question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice, further to the violent attacks and disorder in August 2024, how many cases have still to progress to trial.
answer_text: The Department of Justice does not hold this information; however, the PSNI has submitted 44 files to the Public Prosecution Service (PPS). The PPS is the prosecuting authority in Northern Ireland, responsible for taking decisions as to prosecution in all cases investigated by the police.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice, further to the violent attacks and disorder in August 2024, (i) how many suspects have been charged with membership of a proscribed organisation; (ii) whether any of those cases have been dropped.
answer_text: In relation to the serious disorder in August 2024, no persons have been charged with membership of a proscribed organisation.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the number of staff in his Department that have formally registered an interest because an immediate family member has an agricultural or environment-related commercial interest.
answer_text: The number of staff within my Department who have formally registered an interest because an immediate family member has an agricultural or environment related commercial interest totals 606. Under Departmental guidance, immediate family members include a spouse or partner, in-laws and relations to the extent of parents, siblings, children, and grandparents.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the number of spare drivers there are in each Translink depot.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Translink.  I have sought the requested information and have been advised as follows:In order to operate the Ulsterbus and Metro/Glider timetables each depot requires a set number of drivers to cover the number of driver rotas as well as a number of additional ‘spare’ drivers which are required beyond the rota to cover any periods of absence including; holidays, sickness, training, etc. Ulsterbus generally operate with 13% of spare drivers and Metro/Glider operate with 17%. The number of spare drivers will vary slightly across different depots in Ulsterbus depending on the nature of their timetable commitments throughout the year. For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact Translink directly at aqw.request@translink.co.uk.  Making direct contact with Translink will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, in relation to plans for a new cycling lane at Lagmore Avenue, Belfast, to detail any (i) traffic calming; and (ii) footpath safety measures being put in place at the roadside leading to (a) schools; and (b) residential areas.
answer_text: The Belfast Cycling Network scheme currently in construction will introduce a number of pedestrian and cycling improvements along Lagmore Avenue between Stewartstown Road and Glenfearna Park, enhancing overall active travel provision between local schools and residential areas.The scheme will further enhance already established traffic calming measures on Lagmore Avenue and Drive, with the provision of raised table crossing points at all minor road junctions along Lagmore Avenue to further reduce traffic speeds and provide higher quality crossing facilities for pedestrians. In addition, two new Toucan crossings will be installed along route together with two new uncontrolled pedestrian crossing points near bus stops further along Lagmore Avenue.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the number of failure to operate (i) bus; and (ii) glider journeys in the last five years.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Translink.  I have sought the requested information and have been advised as follows:MetroYearCancellationsReliability202052099.92%2021757699.17%20222493297.27%20231094098.78%20241202798.74%2025217299.04%GliderYearCancellationsReliability20205199.95%202148799.69%202287899.43%2023140299.08%2024107499.29%202531799.14%Please note that due to a virus attack in 2020 the figures for that year are from 1 July.For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact Translink directly at aqw.request@translink.co.uk.  Making direct contact with Translink will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail how frequently the Glider service departs from (i) Dundonald; and (ii) McKinstry Road.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Translink.  I have sought the requested information and have been advised as follows:The frequency of Glider services varies through the day based on levels of demand so there is no single figure to capture the extensive service offering from either departure point, however they are approx. every 8 mins during the peak and 9 min off peak. The Glider timetable can be accessed on the Translink website.For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact Translink directly at aqw.request@translink.co.uk.  Making direct contact with Translink will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the frequency of (i) east bound; and (ii) west bound Belfast city centre (a) bus service; and (b) Glider service departures.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Translink.  I have sought the requested information and have been advised as follows:The current service level for services in Belfast is as follows-Per day for Monday to Friday:Glider (Westbound) 117 departures.Glider (Eastbound) 119 departures.Feeder services (West) 187 departures.Feeder services (East) 193 departures.Metro 10 services (Westbound) 121 departures.Metro 4 services (Eastbound) 76 departures.Metro 3F/G/H services (Eastbound) 32 departures.For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact Translink directly at aqw.request@translink.co.uk.  Making direct contact with Translink will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 24437/22-27, whether the land on which the trees were felled is owned or controlled by her Department.
answer_text: A portion of the tree felling has been carried out within the existing highway boundary controlled by the Department, and has been completed under a licence agreement (permit consent) with the Department. The remainder of the area in which trees have been felled is in the ownership of the housing developer.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any assessment her Department has made in relation to employers' flexibility when employing people with disabilities.
answer_text: My Department has commissioned and published research that explores the barriers that employers face when employing disabled people and people with health conditions. These findings have informed development of a draft Disability and Work Strategy for Northern Ireland, co-produced with employers, containing a number of actions to support and enable employers to employ more disabled people.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for a breakdown of the hospitality spend by Northern Ireland Medical and Dental Training Agency in 2023-24.
answer_text: A breakdown of hospitality expenditure by the Northern Ireland Medical and Dental Training Agency in 2023-24 is set out in the table below.NIMDTA HOSPITALITY EXPENDITURE 2023-24- £’000AprilMayJuneJulyAugSeptOctNovDecJanFebMarchTotal0.46736789776874

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the cost to include Irish language on existing signage at Belfast Grand Central Station.
answer_text: The cost of the new physical signage at the Belfast Grand Central station is estimated to be £120,000.  The estimated cost of changes to the Ticket Vending Machines is £28,000.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the number of (i) arrests; and (ii) prosecutions that were made following the violent attacks and disorder in August 2024.
answer_text: As of 8th April, a total of 48 arrests have been made in relation to the serious public disorder that occurred in August 2024.The PSNI has submitted 44 files to the Public Prosecution Service however, some files refer to multiple suspects.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the impact the Chancellor's Spring Statement will have on public services.
answer_text: The Spring Statement was disappointing as it failed to deliver on what we need the most which is investment in public services. The Spring Statement provided the Executive with £14 million of additional Barnett consequentials, £12.5 million Resource and £1.6 million Capital DEL. Whilst welcome, these additional funds will not address the significant pressures facing our public services. The Chancellor also announced the creation of a £3.25 billion Transformation Fund to support the reform of services in England and Wales. The Barnett consequentials for allocations from this fund in 2025-26 will be notified at Westminster Main Estimates in June, with future years likely to form part of the Spending Review. It will be for the Executive to decide how any additional funding is used.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether his Department will have powers to veto or amend contracts between other Departments and other organisations under its procurement strategy.
answer_text: ANSWERPublic procurement is governed by The Procurement Act 2023 (as amended) which sets out strict rules regarding the award and operation of public contracts.  Procurement regulations aim to ensure that public procurement processes, including award, modification and termination of contracts, are fair, transparent, and non-discriminatory.I intend to bring the draft Public Procurement Policy Statement to the Executive shortly for their consideration.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any human rights obligations that have been considered for inclusion as part of his Department's procurement strategy.
answer_text: ANSWEREach public sector contract has a specific procurement strategy, considered on an individual contract basis and in accordance with relevant procurement policy and legislation. Procurement Policy Note 05/21 Human Rights in Public Procurement, approved by the Executive in November 2021, mandates the actions that Departments must take to incorporate human rights considerations into contracts when conducting a procurement process.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any individuals outside his Department that have been considered to operate the governance and oversight structures as part of its procurement strategy.
answer_text: ANSWERI intend to bring the draft Public Procurement Policy Statement to the Executive shortly for their consideration. Interim governance arrangements for public procurement policy have been developed and will be published with the Public Procurement Policy Statement following approval by the Executive.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance, in light of expected funding cuts to Foras na Gaeilge, to outline any work he has carried out to ensure additional funding is provided to Irish language organisations.
answer_text: In recent years it has become apparent that the inflexible application of the ratios used to fund the North South Bodies, has had a constraining effect usually as a result of the differing financial contexts that Sponsor departments are working in.In response to this issue, my department worked with Southern counterparts to develop an Alternative Funding Proposal which my predecessor Dr Caoimhe Archibald approved as Finance Minister in October 2024. This new arrangement would mean that the Bodies would be able to avail of funding that might have previously been lost to them as a result of the inflexible funding ratio. The proposals seek to address the risk that Bodies will miss out on funding merely due to financial constraints in one jurisdiction.As it is cross cutting, the Alternative Funding proposal requires Executive approval which I have been pressing and will continue to press for. I recently met with my counterpart Jack Chambers TD in the Irish Government and Conradh na Gaeilge, to discuss this matter.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the amount of funding their Department has allocated to initiatives and projects aimed at promoting public participation and deliberative democracy in each year since 2015.
answer_text: The Executive Office does not hold a dedicated budget for participative democracy related activities. In 2016, expenditure of just under £4,000 was incurred on support for the Compact Civic Advisory Panel. There has also been direct public participation in various formats in the development of a range of polices but any costs arising from such participation would be included in the overall expenditure on the particular programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for her assessment of the sustainability of asylum and immigration legal work, given that legal aid rates have not been updated or reformed in over 20 years.
answer_text: As Minister, I am committed to ensuring the most vulnerable within our society have access to legally aided help and representation. I am aware that stakeholder groups working with migrants, refugees and victims of human trafficking have made consistent representation about significant unmet need for legal help for these groups.I announced on 2 December my intention to uplift fees for publicly funded work to ensure fair remuneration and the continued availability of high-quality legal advice and representation as part of my Enabling Access to Justice Reform Programme.  A public consultation process on proposals to amend the criminal, civil and family fees, which have been in place for 10 years, has just concluded and my officials are currently in the process of reviewing responses and preparing a post-consultation report.Officials in my Department are continuing to work closely with immigration practitioners whilst consultation responses are considered to ensure that the proposals, when finalised, will help address the identified need.I have also committed, through the Enabling Access to Justice Delivery Plan, to publish proposals to improve specialist support, including legal support, for migrants, asylum seekers and refugees including those detained at Larne House Immigration Detention Centre to ensure continuing access to legally aided help and representation in future.Between 2021 to 2024, 6301 applications for legal help and representation were granted in relation to asylum and immigration matters with an approved total expenditure of £2.39m.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for his assessment of the ownership of a majority of charged car parks being transferred from local councils to private companies.
answer_text: Car parks is an operational matter for local councils and is outside the remit of my Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he has undertaken an assessment of the impact of capitalism locally on achieving the targets in the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022.
answer_text: The Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022 requires my Department and other departments to undertake a number of assessments, connected to climate action plans and carbon budgets, and to take into consideration a number of factors when setting carbon budgets. However, there are no requirements to assess the impacts of capitalism on achieving the targets in the Act.   As such no assessment has been carried out.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether his Department has considered how the awarding of contracts to Fujitsu will be reflected in the Department’s procurement strategy.
answer_text: ANSWERPublic contracts over the UK Procurement Thresholds are governed by the Procurement Act 2023 (as amended). For services contracts (including IT contracts) the threshold is currently £139,668. As most IT contracts exceed this threshold, it is accepted these contracts will largely be captured under the Procurement Act. The Act sets out the provision for awarding contracts and includes mandatory and discretionary exclusion grounds under which a contracting authority must, or may, exclude a supplier from a competition. The Act also outlines the steps a contracting authority can take depending on what exclusion grounds apply to the supplier and whether the circumstances giving rise to the exclusion ground is continuing or likely to occur again. Consideration of exclusion grounds must be applied on a case by case basis and it is not possible to have a blanket exclusion policy for specific suppliers.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether the Public Procurement Policy Statement has been sent to the Executive for approval.
answer_text: ANSWERFollowing consultation with a wide range of stakeholders I am working with officials to consider the details included in the draft Public Procurement Policy Statement. I intend to bring the draft Public Procurement Policy Statement to the Executive when this is concluded.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any plans to transfer the ownership of charged car parks from private companies back to local councils.
answer_text: Car parks is an operational matter for local councils.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail how schools educate pupils on reproductive health issues, including access to mental and physical healthcare.
answer_text: Relationships and Sexuality Education (RSE) is mandatory for all pupils of compulsory school age.  It is covered in Personal Development and Mutual Understanding at primary level and Personal Development strand of the Learning for Life and Work area of learning at post-primary level.All grant-aided schools are required to have an RSE policy that is based on consultation with parents and pupils.  Subject to meeting minimum legal requirements, it is the responsibility of the Board of Governors of each school to ensure that a comprehensive programme is delivered which meets the needs of its pupils and aligns with its RSE policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her assessment of (i) the delays in delivering the second North South Interconnector; and (ii) the impacts that these delays will have on meeting the target of 80 per cent renewable energy target by 2030.
answer_text: The North South Interconnector Project is an essential part of the North’s decarbonisation, and timelines associated with it are vital.  SONI estimates that the cost to local electricity consumers due to the absence of the interconnector are currently estimated at £19m a year. My Department has recently been informed by SONI that the completion date has been revised from 2028 to 2031.  EirGrid, the system operator in the South of Ireland, has advised that this is due both to delays on processing land access and on actual construction time. The North South Interconnector is an integral part of the network build needed to facilitate the decarbonisation of the electricity network in the North and on the island.  Sufficient and timely build of the network is essential to deliver our 80x30 targets, any delay is therefore of concern and could inhibit our ability to meet the targets. The Department is concerned at the ongoing significant cost implication for electricity customers in the North, and the potential impact on target delivery. Therefore, we are engaging with officials in the Southern Government, to explore options for mitigating or reducing these delays.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 22989/22-27, when the review is expected to be completed.
answer_text: I expect the review to be completed by Invest NI within six months.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any plans to expand physiological and emotional support services for those living with (i) endometriosis; and (ii) premenstrual dysphoric disorder, given that only one Health and Social Care Trust has a specialised team.
answer_text: As you may be aware, my Department has published the Getting It Right First Time (GIRFT) Report for Gynaecology – this signals our commitment to working towards improving services for women which includes those suffering from endometriosis.The GIRFT report has identified 20 general recommendations to improve services through coordinated regional action, as well as a list of specific recommendations for each Health and Social Care (HSC) Trust to take forward. These recommendations have been accepted in principle with a view to implementing the required improvements as soon as it is feasible; however, it is acknowledged that some recommendations will require additional funding and lead-in time to deliver fully.A Task and Finish Group to develop and implement an approved pathway for endometriosis has been established, and work is underway with key stakeholders to provide a holistic improved pathway for women.  Trusts have advised that their current services have the following psychological and emotional support services available for those with a diagnosis of endometriosis.Belfast Trust have an Endometriosis Clinical Nurse Specialist who can provide emotional support for women diagnosed with endometriosis. Patients can be referred to general psychological and pain services for further support although it is recognised that these services also have significant waiting time.In Southern Trust there is currently no dedicated psychological and emotional support services available for those with a diagnosis of endometriosis.In South Eastern Trust there is no dedicated psychology service for women suffering from endometriosis.  There is however access to the chronic pain team but again this is not a dedicated service.Northern Trust does not have any specific or ring-fenced psychological or emotional support services for women with a diagnosis of endometriosis. If indicated, a referral to general psychological services can be made by the attending clinical team.The Western Trust would advise that they are not currently commissioned to provide specific psychological support to patient diagnosed with Endometriosis.  Patients can however contact the Endometriosis Nurse Specialist, who can provide emotional support. The Nurse Specialist can also sign-posted patients to services within the community or back to their GP for community-based support.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail any research her Department has completed on the role the media can play in increasing young men’s propensity to commit gender-based violence.
answer_text: My Department has worked in partnership with The Executive Office on the PSNI-led ‘Power to Change’ campaign that launched in January this year. As part of this, we provided funding to the PSNI to allow them to commission market research to inform the campaign. This research included interviews with key stakeholders and qualitive focus groups that explored public perceptions of and responses to different forms of gender-based violence and unwanted behaviours; as well as views on mindsets and attitudes that need to be challenged in Northern Ireland.The Power to Change campaign aims to tackle the underpinning behaviours and attitudes that can fuel sexual offending and gender-based violence, including violence against women and girls. EPPOC's latest 'Ending the Harm' campaign, which was launched in October 2024, highlights a number of ways that paramilitaries exploit and harm vulnerable people and communities. We know from academic research and programme insight the effect of paramilitary harm on women and girls. We also know that the coercion and intimidation that often comes with this makes victims feel that they have nowhere to turn to for help. To that end, one of the new campaign creatives specifically says "Paramilitaries abuse and control women and girls'. The intention is that, by highlighting the issue, we can encourage more victims to come forward for help and support.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for his assessment of the gorse fires at Altnadua that occurred around St. Patrick’s Day 2025.
answer_text: It is estimated that 10ha of gorse and scrub was burnt. There are no environmental designations such as ASSI on the site. The fire is believed to have been started deliberately.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the number of notifications they have received from farmers before they have commenced a gorse fire in each of the last five years.
answer_text: The standing advice from both my Department and the Northern Ireland Fire and Rescue Service (NIFRS) to farmers and land managers is that they should notify NIFRS when they commence a prescribed burn together with details of where the prescribed burn is taking place and when they have finished burning. I am aware that NIFRS has received such notifications. My Department does not hold this information.			Except in relation to ASSIs there is no mandatory or legal requirement for farmers or land managers to give notification to the Department in advance of any planned prescribed burning. 		It is a legal requirement that farmers or land managers apply for consent to undertake prescribed burning when the activity is to be undertaken within an ASSI. 		The Department has received six applications for consent to carry out prescribed burning over the last five years.		The Department undertook a consultation on Wildfires in Northern Ireland, Draft Strategic Way Forward in 2023, including the area of Policy, Legislation and Enforcement. A planned new approach is currently in development with a view to publication in the time ahead.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the policing and justice protocols when a gorse fire is reported.
answer_text: The operational lead on gorse fires is the Northern Ireland Fire and Rescue Service (NIFRS), with police providing support in terms of evacuations. Gorse fires are a serious threat to both the environment and public safety. These fast-spreading blazes can cause significant damage to farmland, destroy vital wildlife habitats, place lives and property at risk and place an immense strain on our emergency services. When investigating gorse fires, as the result of accidental negligence or intentional arson, it is important that the Police Service of Northern Ireland and NIFRS work together to investigate suspicious fires, gather evidence and pursue prosecutions.If the gorse fire was so significant that it was declared a Major Incident by Police, then the wider Major Incident Response Plan would be instigated. This would be a multi-agency response to discharge responsibilities under the Civil Contingencies Act 2004.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the number of prosecutions for gorse fires in each of the last five years.
answer_text: There is no offence relating specifically to the setting of gorse fires, other than ‘burning whins in close season’.  During the period 2020 – 2024, there were no prosecutions at courts for the offence ‘burning whins in close season’.   The setting of gorse fires more generally may be prosecuted as ‘arson’.  However, Departmental prosecutions and convictions datasets do not contain information on the circumstances in which such an offence occurred, or in relation to the type of property damaged by such an act.  It is therefore not possible to directly answer the question posed from information held.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail what legislation exists in relation to housing standards.
answer_text: The minimum fitness standard for human habitation is set out in Article 46 of the Housing (NI) Order 1981 and applies to all housing tenures.The legislation governing standards in the private rented sector is the Private Tenancies (Northern Ireland) Order 2006; The Smoke, Heat, and Carbon Monoxide Alarms for Private Tenancies Regulations (NI) 2024; and The Electrical Safety Standards for Private Tenancies Regulations (NI) 2024.The Houses in Multiple Occupation Act (Northern Ireland) 2016 also sets out specific standards for those type of properties.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for her assessment of the impact that debt recovery notices, resulting from effectively unenforceable private parking fines, have on the mental health of vulnerable people.
answer_text: Whilst my Department is responsible for on-street parking enforcement across the north, it has no responsibility for the operation and management of private car parks nor the enforcement companies that operate within them.I understand that the Consumer Protection Team of the Consumer Council offers advice and assistance on matters relating to private car parks.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the amount her Department's active travel section has spent on consultancy fees in the last five years.
answer_text: My Department’s Active Travel team engage engineering consultancy firms to provide managed professional services in the development and delivery of its capital works programme primarily through the Engineering Services Consultancy Partnering Contract and, when appropriate, through the Major Works and Transport Planning Frameworks.In the last five years to end of 2023/24 the expenditure on the above has been approximately £4m.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any IT support that the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust can provide to the charity sector.
answer_text: The Belfast Trust Digital Services department provides IT and Telecommunications services to support the clinical and operational service delivery of the Trust, including the provision of certain regional services.  They are not currently funded or commissioned to provided services to the voluntary or charitable sector.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether Translink completed any cost-benefit analysis exercises prior to taking the decision to import hydrogen to fuel buses.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Translink.  I have sought the requested information and have been advised as follows:Translink operate commercial contracts for the supply of hydrogen. These contracts were competitively tendered and ensure continuity of supply and regular delivery of hydrogen to Translink sites. The business case for the wider programme for the purchase of the vehicles took into consideration the cost benefits of the programme. The supplier is responsible for the decision making in regards to completing a cost - benefit analysis regarding the sourcing and supply of the hydrogen to fuel the buses. For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact Translink directly at aqw.request@translink.co.uk.  Making direct contact with Translink will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 23574/22-27, whether Translink carried out any environmental impact assessments prior to taking the decision to import hydrogen to fuel buses.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Translink.  I have sought the requested information and have been advised as follows:Translink operate commercial contracts for the supply of hydrogen. These contracts were competitively tendered and ensure continuity of supply and regular delivery of hydrogen to Translink sites. The business case for the wider programme for the purchase of the vehicles took into consideration the cost benefits of the programme. The supplier is responsible for the decision making in regards to completing a cost - benefit analysis regarding the sourcing and supply of the hydrogen to fuel the buses. For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact Translink directly at aqw.request@translink.co.uk.  Making direct contact with Translink will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Assembly Commission for its assessment of the use of automated cleaning services by Aramark in Parliament Buildings.

To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on his plans to support people on the Personal Independent Payment and Universal Credit following the British Government's recent announcement of changes to the benefits system.
To ask the Minister of Finance, pursuant to AQW 20038/22-27, whether he has received a response from Treasury in relation to a change in treatment of Housing Executive borrowing.
answer_text: The Chief Secretary of the Treasury wrote to Dr Caoimhe Archibald MLA, as the Minister of Finance, on 24 January 2025.  He advised that while he recognised Housing Executive borrowing to be an important issue, it should be discussed as part of the negotiations towards a full Fiscal Framework. I raised the issue of Housing Executive borrowing at a recent Finance Inter-Ministerial Standing Committee (F:ISC) meeting with the Chief Secretary on 27 February 2025  and will continue to press Treasury on this matter.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether Invest NI has provided funding to Tesla in the last ten years.
answer_text: Invest NI has not provided any funding to Tesla within the last ten years.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the number of hate crimes recorded against (i) transgender; and (ii) non-binary communities in the last five years.
answer_text: Information on number of hate crimes reported to police against transgender or non-binary communities is a matter for the Chief Constable, who is accountable to the Northern Ireland Policing Board.  I am committed to respecting the operational independence of the Chief Constable and the role of the Northern Ireland Policing Board.You may, therefore, wish to direct any query relating to the issues raised in the question to the PSNI.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the number of people who were refused Personal Independence Payment that died within (i) six months; and (ii) 12 months of the refusal.
answer_text: My Department does not hold this information.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when his Department will publish its updated analysis of the cost of implementing the new welfare mitigations outlined in the Welfare Mitigations Review - Independent Advisory Panel report.
answer_text: The refreshed estimated costs associated with the recommendations in the Independent Advisory Panel Report into the future of welfare mitigations were shared with the Executive and the Communities Committee on 10 March 2025, and presented to the Assembly on 13 March 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail his plans to protect people from the welfare benefit cuts announced by the British Government.
answer_text: I refer the member to the answer provided to AQW 24594/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the number of appeals for Personal Independence Payment were successful in each of the last five years.
answer_text: The table below shows the number of PIP appeals that were successful in the last five years.YearsPIP Appeal Decision more advantageous to the appellant2019/20203,7692020/20217782021/20224552022/20232,2772023/20241,1872024-Nov 2024670

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the funding allocated to Capita in the last ten years.
answer_text: In the last ten years the Department has paid Capita £182,868,994 for the delivery of benefit related health assessments.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any consideration he has given to ending the welfare contracts with Capita in order to secure funding to protect people from the British Government’s recently announced welfare benefit cuts.
answer_text: I have given no consideration to ending the Functional Assessment Service contract with Capita on the basis that, a Business Case previously concluded that a single external provider represented best value for money, as in-housing the service would cost an additional £93m over a ten-year period. The Department does however complete PIP award reviews in-house where sufficient evidence is available.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any consideration he has given to introducing a contingency fund to protect citizens from the British Government’s recently announced welfare benefit cuts.
answer_text: I am extremely concerned about the proposed welfare changes announced on 18 March 2025, which I raised during a meeting I had with Hilary Benn, Secretary of State for Northern Ireland, on 20 March 2025. 		I understand the shock and uncertainty caused by the proposed changes, but my Department does not yet know the full impact. 		My officials are continuing to work closely with the Department for Work and Pensions to understand each of the changes and their implications for Northern Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) to detail why Belfast Harbour is seeking reclassification as a private corporation; and (ii) for her assessment of the implications this will have on Belfast Harbour’s real estate and land holding operations.
answer_text: As a Trust Port, Belfast Harbour is currently classified by the Office of National Statistics (ONS) as a public corporation for accounting purposes only. This affects their ability to borrow, as any debt they take on counts against my Department’s Capital Delegated Expenditure Limit (CDEL) budget. The reclassification from Public Non-Financial Corporations to Private Non-Financial Corporations, would be an ONS classification change, with no legal change in the status of the Harbour.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 23496/22-27, whether green or low carbon hydrogen will be purchased from local companies to fuel Translink’s hydrogen buses.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Translink.  I have sought the requested information and have been advised as follows:The requested information regarding where Translink sources its hydrogen gas supplies is regarded as commercially sensitive.              For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact Translink directly at aqw.request@translink.co.uk.  Making direct contact with Translink will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department is adopting a gender budget approach to ensure gynaecology health needs are being met.
answer_text: There are significant challenges with waiting lists and waiting times for gynaecology services.  However, there is currently considerable work underway across the Health and Care System to try to bring improvements in this specialty, although the current financial position does make progress difficult.A formal Gender Budget approach is not taken however I can confirm that when planning all services, my officials take account of Equality and Human Rights Legislation as part of the development of budgets and business cases.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for his assessment of the scale of medical misogyny across health and social care.
answer_text: I welcome the recent report by the UK Parliament Women and Equalities Committee on women’s reproductive health conditions which highlights that women often find their pain is normalised or dismissed when seeking medical help in the NHS in England.  Whilst I fully acknowledge these concerns, as this is not measured or quantified it is not possible to comment on the scale of this issue across health and social care in Northern Ireland.  The report enables informed discussion and aims to improve these experiences by placing a focus on the need to improve clinical education on reproductive health conditions, particularly in primary care, as a means of delivering better health outcomes for women. My Department recently undertook engagement with women’s representative groups and healthcare professionals as part of ongoing work to develop a women’s health action plan.  The qualitative feedback received through this engagement reflects similar experiences among some women in Northern Ireland to those in England, while others have reported having a positive experience when discussing issues with their healthcare provider.  My Department is currently considering the feedback received and how the action plan might (subject to resources) address these concerns, for example, through targeted clinical education about conditions affecting women’s health and wellbeing and improving awareness and understanding among the wider public to help remove the stigma associated with these health conditions.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail his plans to reduce gynaecology waiting list times.
answer_text: The recent Getting It Right First Time (GIRFT) Report on gynaecology services in Northern Ireland has led to several changes and initiatives aimed at improving Gynaecology services in Northern Ireland.The report includes 20 regional recommendations designed to improve patient care and tackle waiting times, along with bespoke recommendations for each Health and Social Care Trust.The Department of Health has established a regional forum with Trust, GP and PHA representation to oversee ongoing service improvement in this speciality and ensure that this improvement is implemented at a local and regional level.  As part of the reforms my officials have also established a number of Task and Finish Groups to consider, develop and standardise approved regional pathways for conditions such as Endometriosis, Menopause, Heavy Menstrual Loss, Recurrent Pregnancy Loss, Fertility and Urogynae with Mesh.Already a number of service improvements have been realised, including the development of a Red Flag Pathway, which is to be ratified by stakeholders imminently. Waiting times have also started to demonstrate improvements as services get back to full capacity, in recent months.As well as the regional work underway Trusts have also been asked to establish their own action plans to take forward recommendations and improvements which are relevant to their Trust. My officials from the Strategic Planning and Performance Group are currently in the process of writing to Trust’s to ask for an update to their action plans.Some of the recommendations will require investment, whilst others will be improved through modernisation and reform.

answer_text: According to HMRC’s regional trade statistics, the North’s goods exports to the United States were £1.68 billion in 2024 and imports were £0.75 billion. question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the rationale behind the recent tree-felling at Caw roundabout, Derry.
answer_text: The tree felling at Caw Roundabout was carried out by the developer of the adjacent H30 housing development at Rosses Gate, Ballyoan.

answer_text: Expenditure to Kingsbridge Healthcare Group by (i) my Department and (ii) Health and Social Care Trusts in each of the last ten years, is set out in the table below.Expenditure incurred is primarily for waiting list initiatives for red flag and urgent cases, and assessment and treatment of new out patients and has delivered over 138,000 appointments over the 10-year period. question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the number of street cleansing workers in Belfast City Council not engaged in bin collections in each of the last five years.
answer_text: Street cleansing and bin collections are operational matters for local councils.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any plans her Department has in place if the President of the United States of America implements tariffs on goods which impacts the Northern Ireland economy.
answer_text: The US is an important trade and investment market for the economy in the North, therefore the threat of tariffs is concerning. Tariffs are a barrier to trade and are bad for business. I will continue to monitor developments and work with all partners to highlight the need to protect our economy, and to advocate on behalf of trade with and investment in the North.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any statutory duty upon his Department to fund the Scoil Spreagtha programme.
answer_text: The Department of Education has a statutory duty to encourage and facilitate the development of Irish-medium education, as per Article 89 of the Education (Northern Ireland) Order 1998. This article also states “Irish-medium education” means education provided in an Irish speaking school.Scoil Spreagtha is described by Gael Linn as “an accredited scheme to recognise and celebrate efforts made by English-medium schools to encourage the use of Irish in the school environment”. This scheme is for English-medium schools. As such, this does not fall under the Department of Education’s statutory duties.To date, my Department has not been involved in the Scoil Spreagtha project nor has it provided any funding.  Education continues to face unavoidable cost pressures and rising service demands associated with delivering statutory and policy obligations.  I am, therefore, not in a position to commit funding to this scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether he has had any discussions in relation to placing a requirement on ATM providers to not charge users to withdraw their money before allowing them to avail of the ATM rates relief scheme.
answer_text: ANSWER	The power within the Rates (Northern Ireland) Order 1977 for the Rural ATM exemption cannot be used to limit the exemption to machines where “no charge” is applied. 		During previous consultation on the rural ATMs rates exemption it was proposed that it should only apply to free-to-use ATMs, but the views expressed from consultees was that a fee-paying ATM was better than no ATM. On that basis it was agreed that the power to provide an exemption should apply to all ATMs within a designated rural area.  		I have had no discussions with ATM providers since I became Finance Minister last month.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail whether minutes will be taken at all meetings attended by the deputy First Minister during the visit to the United States of America for St. Patrick's Day, including whether the minutes will be publicly available.
answer_text: Minutes of Ministers meetings are recorded in line with our obligations under the Functioning of Government Act.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any assessment he or his officials have made of the Scoil Spreagtha programme not being funded.
answer_text: My Department has not been involved in the Scoil Spreagtha project nor has it provided any funding.  Therefore, no such assessment has been carried out.Given the unavoidable financial pressures and increasing demands on education services, my priority remains fulfilling statutory and policy obligations.  I am, therefore, not in a position to commit funding to this scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of meetings (i) his Department; and (ii) the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust held with Graham-Bam Healthcare Partnership ahead of them being awarded the contract for new Children’s Hospital.
answer_text: The contract for the new Children’s Hospital was awarded to John Graham Construction Limited following the withdrawal of BAM Construction from the Graham BAM Healthcare Partnership. Works commenced on site on 3 March 2025.The number of meetings, following full business case approval in July 2024 and in advance of site commencement, with the contractor were as follows:With the Department of Health; fifty-eight meetings, of which nineteen were led by DoH officials and a further thirty-nine led by the design team and attended by DoH officials. Belfast Health and Social Care Trust officials were present at some of these meetings.With the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust:An additional three meetings were led by Belfast Health and Social Care Trust with DoH officials not in attendance.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether all calls to the Housing Executive are recorded.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive. I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows: “The Housing Executive currently do not record all calls. However, following a recent call recording pilot, the Housing Executive is planning to introduce this facility to all staff working in telephony roles.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Housing Executive directly. Making direct contact with the ALB will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any discussions he has had with the Department for Work and Pensions in relation to reported plans by the UK Government to reduce welfare payments.
answer_text: I have raised my concerns about the UK Government’s proposed welfare changes directly with the Secretary of State for Northern Ireland and the Department for Work and Pensions Minister for Social Security and Disability, Sir Stephen Timms MP. I also raised concerns, particularly with reference to Personal Independence Payment, at the March 2025 meeting of the Four Nations Ministerial Group on Child Poverty which was chaired by the UK’s Minister for School Standards.My officials are engaging with officials in the Department for Work and Pensions to understand any proposed changes to welfare payments and will assess the impact of any such changes for Northern Ireland.  This engagement is at an early stage.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 21785/22-27, whether he or his Department have any concerns about the health impact on children who are living in temporary accommodation.
answer_text: I refer the member to AQW 21785/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 21944/22-27, to detail the number of cases of damp and mould found in homes in the private rented sector in each of the last five years.
answer_text: My Department does not have this information.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 22234/22-27, to detail the proportion of the figures provided that cover payments made to landlords.
answer_text: The proportion of the housing component of Universal Credit (UC) that covered payments made to landlords in each of the last five years is shown in the table below.  Expenditure2019/202020/212021/222022/232023/24Universal Credit Housing Element71%68%70%71%72%NotesData Source: Universal Credit Full Service (UCFS) system - 2019 to 2024.Universal Credit figures refer to the proportion of the total amount of Housing Element that households in payment are entitled to receive that was agreed to be paid directly to landlords. Universal Credit housing element is paid at household level.Figures have been rounded to the nearest 1 percent.It is not possible to detail the proportion of the Housing Benefit figures provided in AQW 22234/22-27 that covers payments made to landlords as the Department does not hold readily accessible records that would provide a breakdown of Housing Benefit payments issued, split between individual claimants and landlords in the private tenant and housing association categories.  The Department can confirm that Housing Benefit payments for Housing Executive tenants are paid directly to the landlord and that the vast majority of housing associations also receive Housing Benefit payments directly; however, Housing Benefit payments for tenants living in the private rented sector can be claimant or landlord paid, but it is not possible to detail the proportion that are paid directly to the landlord.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any consideration his officials have given to a new site for Gaelscoil An Lonnáin Primary School.
answer_text: My department has no plans to relocate Gaelscoil An Lonnáin at this time.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any discussions she or her officials have had with the Utility Regulator to ensure compensation payments were made by NIE Networks to those impacted by Storm Éowyn.
answer_text: Following disruption and damage caused by Storm Éowyn on 24th of January, a working group involving the Department for the Economy, NIE Networks, and the Utility Regulator, was established to consider compensation payments for electricity customers most affected by power outages. The working group was established at the request of the Executive to consider options where compensation payments would be recovered through customer electricity bills. The working group has now updated the Executive of its findings that there is currently no existing route to make payments to customers. NIE Networks has applied a severe weather exemption provided for in legislation under which customers can apply for compensation if they have been off electricity supply. The working group concluded that any other options involving recovery of payments from network charges would require legal or regulatory modifications which cannot be applied retrospectively for those customers impacted by Storm Éowyn.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether the total cost of the new Children’s Hospital has been allocated to his Department.
answer_text: The total cost of the new Children’s Hospital has not been allocated to my Department as the project spans several financial years.An allocation of £28.9 million was made to my Department in financial year 2024/25 for the Mother and Children’s flagship project.  The Draft Budget document sets out the Northern Ireland Executive’s proposed spending plans for the one-year period from 1 April 2025 to 31 March 2026, and proposes an allocation of £69.8 million for the Mother and Children’s flagship project.  No budget allocation has been made to my Department for 2025/26 as the final budget has not yet been confirmed.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the investment in mental health services for young people in West Belfast in the last five years.
answer_text: Regrettably, neither my Department nor the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust hold financial data in a manner that would enable us to identify investment specific to West Belfast.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether his Department has a working definition of the term failing civil servant.
answer_text: There is no working definition of the term “failing civil servant”. The Civil Service Performance Management policy defines performance as “unsatisfactory” where a civil servant’s overall performance is unacceptable and the achievement of their objectives and/or demonstration of competencies falls clearly short of the standard required.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the average service charge for Housing Association tenants in the last five years.
answer_text: My Department does not hold this information. This is an operational matter for Registered Housing Associations.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether he will consider expanding the Bright Start programme.
answer_text: The final opening budget for the 2025-2026 financial year has not been agreed by the Executive.  It is hoped that this will be agreed shortly after the closure of the consultation on the overall budget on 13 March, and in advance of the new financial year.  Once agreed, I will be in a position to finalise my Budget Strategy and confirm funding for my Departmental programmes.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 23656/22-27, to detail (i) the relevant oil products; and (ii) the duration of outage or shortage.
answer_text: Further to AQW 23656/22-27, our three local oil terminals (Puma, Exolum and LSS) have confirmed that there has not been an oil product outage in the last five years.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether the UK Government has engaged with him or his officials over the reported policy which plans to dismiss failing civil servants.
answer_text: ANSWERThere has been no engagement with me or my officials on this issue.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for his assessment of the financial and operational sustainability of childcare providers funded through Bright Start.
answer_text: The Department for the Economy (DfE) is leading on a scoping exercise to develop a clearer picture of the business support requirements of the childcare sector.  This work has involved direct engagement with childcare providers, including those currently funded through the Bright Start School Age Childcare Grant Scheme, to better understand the financial challenges they face and consider what an appropriate government response should be.  This work is due to conclude by the end of March 2025, and will inform the development of the draft Early Learning and Childcare Strategy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the membership of the panel responsible for scoring core grant funding applications.
answer_text: Assessment Panels for the 2025/26 Core Grant Scheme have been chaired by a Grade 7 senior manager within the Department, supported by two separate officials from the Staff Officer or Deputy Principal Grade.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice, in light of the £2.19 million public transformation funding for her Department's modernisation of electronic monitoring proposal, to detail (i) who controls the GPS data on individuals; and (ii) where the information is stored.
answer_text: The allocation of funding for this project was confirmed by the Department of Finance on 4 March 2025. Work will shortly commence to establish a dedicated team to take this forward.  As part of this project, detailed consideration will be given to the arrangements for the handling and storage of data derived from the use of GPS/location monitoring technology.   Further information will therefore be available after the project begins and when data management arrangements are more fully developed.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for his assessment of the impact of the loss of core grant funding to 94 per cent of applicants on (i) the service users and communities these organisations support; and (ii) the stability of the wider community and voluntary sector.
answer_text: In July 2024 I announced that the Core Grant scheme would be redesigned in order to address the unfairness in the previous scheme which had funded the same organisations for more than 20 years.This new scheme was designed last year in partnership with the community and voluntary sector in a process facilitated by the Northern Ireland Council for Voluntary Action.This redesign process was open to all community and voluntary sector organisations and included several engagement events which community and voluntary organisations attended and participated in, leading to a number of agreed “key elements” for the scheme being proposed to me. I agreed to all of those elements. 61 of the 259 applicants for core grant in 2025/26 were previously in receipt of core grant, of which 47 were unsuccessful. These 47 organisations which are therefore experiencing a loss of core grant from 2024/25 to 2025/26 representing 18% of applicants.  It is therefore, incorrect to suggest 94% of applicants “lost” core funding.While core grant can assist organisations with financial stability, it has never directly funded the provision of health and social care services and therefore should not have a significant impact upon these services. Indeed, the majority of my Department’s investment in the community and voluntary sector is through HSC service delivery contracts, which are separate to the Core Grant Scheme. Despite the ongoing budget challenges facing my Department, I remain committed to supporting the community and voluntary sector, and where the wider health allocation allows me to increase the scheme budget in future years I will do so.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he will conduct an equality impact assessment in relation to his Department’s decision to maintain the 50 per cent cut to core grant funding, which was originally made in 2023.
answer_text: The delivery of the Department’s Core Grant Funding Scheme has been based on 12-month agreements with the Community and Voluntary organisations involved. Each year applicants have been advised that funding was subject to the Department’s final budget allocation each year.  There has therefore never been any guarantee of funding from one year to the next. In July 2024 I announced that the old Core Grant Scheme would be redesigned in order to address the unfairness in the previous scheme, which had funded the same organisations for more than 20 years.The new Scheme for 2025/26 was designed last year in partnership with the Community and Voluntary sector in a process facilitated by NICVA, the Northern Ireland Council for Voluntary Action, and relaunched in Autumn 2024. When I invited applications I made it clear that I expected to be able to find at least £1.8m for the Scheme in 2025/26, and I regret that the indicative budget for Health does not permit any increase to that figure.While Core Grant can make a contribution towards financial stability, it has never directly funded the provision of health and social care services and therefore should not have a significant impact upon any users of these services. Indeed, the majority of my Department’s investment in the Community and Voluntary Sector is through HSC service delivery contracts, which are separate to the Core Grant Scheme. Despite the ongoing budget challenges facing my Department, I remain committed to supporting the Community and Voluntary Sector, and where the wider Health allocation allows me to increase the Scheme budget in future years I will do so.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the number of individuals in the Northern Ireland Civil Service that have availed of the Scottish Independent Appeal panel for a complaint against a senior colleague in the last ten years.
answer_text: ANSWERMy officials are not aware of any cases being referred to the Scottish Independent Appeal Panel.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the planned capital spend on new build or maintenance for youth provision services in the 2025-26 financial year.
answer_text: Unfortunately, my Department continues to face significant pressures across all capital programmes. Once my Department’s budget is confirmed, I will consider youth capital requirements against my Department’s other priorities.The Department is currently unable to provide capital funding towards maintenance or repair works to voluntary youth organisations.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, in light of the £61 million public transformation funding allocated to his Department's Primary Care Multidisciplinary Team proposal, to detail any work that will be carried out to ensure this includes access to mental health support in GP services.
To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the number of recorded cases of red-categorised risks that the Northern Ireland Civil Service has dealt with in the last ten years.
answer_text: ANSWERMy Department manages risks through its Risk Management Framework, in line with standard practice. Each business area maintains a risk register, which is updated regularly and, where appropriate, significant risks are escalated to the appropriate level. The number of red risks over the past 10 years is not a metric that has been collected.Individual departmental risk registers fall under the responsibility of each respective Accounting Officer.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail how she intends to address the issue of public transport from Parliament Buildings finishing at 6:10pm in the evening, given that sittings often finish later than this time.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Translink.  I have sought the requested information and have been advised as follows:The Public Service Agreement allows Translink to amend services but they must do so in a cost-effective manner, consistent with the required level of quality and performance, and minimise costs.  Any enhancement of the 3G service would require additional funding however, Translink has advised that an alternative is to use the Glider service on the Newtownards Road from the Stormont stop. Services run until just after 11pm.For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact Translink directly at aqw.request@translink.co.uk.  Making direct contact with Translink will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any economic appraisal that has been made in relation to investing in a capital build of St. Peter’s Immaculata Youth Club.
answer_text: Unfortunately, my Department continues to face significant pressures across all capital programmes. Given these demands, no capital funding was available to progress any youth schemes to site in the 2023/24 or 2024/25 financial years. This has meant the Education Authority has been unable to finalise the feasibility study and commence work on the business case for St Peter’s Immaculata Youth Centre.Once my Department’s budget is confirmed, I will consider youth capital requirements against my Department’s other capital priorities.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether her Department is responsible for the recent felling of a significant area of trees at the Caw roundabout, Derry.
answer_text: My Department was not responsible for the felling of trees at the southeastern side of the Caw Roundabout adjacent to Cooney Road and Crescent Link. This work was undertaken by the developer of the H30 housing development at Rosses Gate, Ballyoan.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when the Protect Life 2 Action Plan will be published.
answer_text: The Protect Life 2 Action Plan and accompanying Implementation Plan will be papers presented at the Executive Working Group on 3 April 2025. Subject to agreement at that meeting, both documents will be published shortly afterwards.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any early intervention considerations he has given to investing in youth services in the Divis and Lower Falls area.
answer_text: The Education Authority Youth Service develops Youth Services based on robust regional and local assessments of need. In relation to consideration for early intervention youth services in the Divis and Lower Falls area, the need for these services was established through the West Belfast Local Assessment of Need and outlined in the West Belfast Local Youth Development Plan.This process includes:Review of the Regional Assessment of Need Survey results per Council and District Electoral Area (DEA)Review of the Assessment of Need Focus Group engagement per Council and DEAReview of the relevant statistical data per Council area and where available DEAWider stakeholder engagement and assessment of relevant research The Education Authority Youth Service currently fund four Youth Providers in the Community and Voluntary Sector in these areas, to deliver early intervention services, through Local Area Based and Local Project funding. Namely, Clonard Monastery Youth Centre, Divis Youth Project, St. Peters Immaculata Youth Centre, and Roden Street Youth Service. Local Area Based funding provides unrestricted access to young people in the Divis and Lower Falls area and includes safe spaces for young people to participate in fun, recreational activities. These facilities also provide group work based on the needs of children and young people, outdoor learning, leadership programmes and accredited youth work programmes.  Early intervention services that are being provided through Education Authority Youth Service Local Project funding in Court DEA are based on the themes of Health and Wellbeing, Learning and Achieving, Community Relations Equality and Diversity, Inclusion and Living in Safety and Stability. Issues that the Youth Service can have a positive impact on in relation to early intervention through local project funding include:Incidences of substance use, particularly in public spaces, resulting in increased vulnerability to harm leading to young people becoming disenfranchised and isolated from the local communityUnhealthy relationships and issues of consent resulting in increased risk to physical and mental wellbeing.Vulnerability in relation to exposure to community violence and car crime resulting in young people not feeling safe.Vulnerability in relation to exposure to violence within the community or home resulting in young people not feeling safe.Lack of educational attainment and low skills resulting in poor life choices and low aspirations resulting in increased social exclusion and isolationThere is potential for coercion into anti-community activities and in particular at times of social unrest.There is a lack of shared educational space within a segregated and densely populated area, exacerbated by interface walls or gates as boundaries, in particular Lanark Way, Northumberland Street and GrosvenorProviding alternative safe spaces for young people, in particular Lanark Way, Northumberland Street and GrosvenorYoung people are participatin
