question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether there is a process in place to determine the number of Housing Executive succession tenancy cases that (i) result in a court hearing; and (ii) result in the tenancy being lost by the appellant.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive.  I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“There is no specific process in place to determine the number of Housing Executive succession of tenancy cases which result in a court case. By extension (assuming that the use of the word appellant in this context means a person in possession of the property who contests court proceedings with a view to becoming the tenant) there is no process to specifically collate the outcome of such cases.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Northern Ireland Housing Executive directly. Making direct contact with the Northern Ireland Housing Executive will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the number of court cases that have taken place between tenants and housing associations in each of the last five years.
answer_text: A manual trawl of court records would be required to determine the number of court cases between tenants and housing associations, this would incur a disproportionate cost.The table below details the number of ejectment cases received and disposed in each of the last five years.  The figures include ejectment actions involving tenants of the NI Housing Executive, housing associations and private landlords.20192020202120222023Received1,164441342746817Disposed936257284439631Source:  Judicial Statistics - https://www.justice-ni.gov.uk/publications/nicts-judicial-statistics

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, in relation to the additional £19.5 million capital DEL allocated in October Monitoring to NI Water to address constraints in wastewater infrastructure, to detail what the £7 million Upper Falls Wastewater Treatment Works Stormwater Storage will be spent on.
answer_text: This is an operational matter for NI Water however I have requested the information and NI Water has advised that the £7 million investment work being proposed involves modifying some of the disused wastewater tanks at the Upper Falls Wastewater Treatment Works site to provide additional stormwater retention and further reduce stormwater discharges to the Blackstaff River.  This further reduction will compensate for additional flows from new development in the Upper Falls area, facilitating the progression of 7 new developments and will provide infrastructure capacity for 1200 residential and 5 commercial units.  The work also involves replacing an existing pumping station which is required to pump the additional retained stormwater back into the sewerage system during dry weather.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to provide details of the legal funds that  can be availed of by victims and survivors of crime.
answer_text: Victims and survivors of crime are eligible to apply for civil legal aid to provide them with legal advice and assistance.  Legal aid is also available on application to provide representation to help victims and survivors of crime to bring or respond to civil legal proceedings relating to the crimes committed against them.  Such applications are subject to means and merits tests in the usual way.Depending on their circumstances, victims may also benefit from the services of Victims Support NI who provide, for example, support for witnesses attending court, access to Sexual Offences Legal Advisers (SOLAs) and help with securing compensation, in addition to providing access to emotional support and practical advice on recovery.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the number of applications for Legal Aid in relation to an applicant challenging the Housing Executive over refusal to grant a tenancy succession that have been declined in each of the last five years.
answer_text: The Legal Services Agency does not classify cases in the manner set out in this question. However, the table below sets out cases in which applicants sought to bring proceedings against the Northern Ireland Housing Executive. Application status20202021202220232024Application requests granted113310Application requests refused 14935Source: LAMS Case Management SystemThe figures presented constitute Management Information and may be subject to change. If an application is refused, a fresh application can be submitted and may be granted.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any role the security services have in the (i) administration; and (ii) determination of the Victims' Payment Scheme.
answer_text: The Department of Justice is responsible for the administration of the Scheme.  The Victims’ Payments Board (the Board) is responsible for determining applications under the Scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance, pursuant to AQW 20131/22-27, for her assessment of whether current public procurement policy permits public bodies to procure goods and services from areas that are illegally occupied, in violation of international law.
answer_text: Procurement Policy Notes (PPNs) which apply to departments and their sponsored bodies when procuring goods, services and works include Procurement Policy Note (PPN) 03/21, this covers Supply Chain Resilience and requires departments to map supply chains for critical supplies contracts to identify risks of potential supply chain disruption; human rights or modern slavery risks. PPN 05/21 relates to Human Rights considerations requiring departments to assess the level of risk involved in each contract to ensure human rights are respected.  The Procurement Contracts Regulations 2015 includes provisions to exclude economic operators who are convicted of specific offences.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs why his Department has not consulted the public on the remediation of Mobuoy illegal dump, given that officials first advised that this consultation would take place before Christmas 2023.
answer_text: The public consultation is an important step and there has been significant work ongoing to prepare for this. The consultation will give all interested parties the opportunity to comment on the draft strategy, I have approved that we move to public consultation and we intend to launch the consultation in early spring.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the reasons why the clean up of the Mobuoy illegal dump has not been included in the draft Programme for Government.
answer_text: The draft Programme for Government (PfG) does not include everything that departments are working to deliver. The absence of a policy from the PfG does not mean that work has stopped or that it is unimportant to the Minister responsible for it.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) the evidence that informed the Executive’s decision to indefinitely ban puberty blockers for under 18s; (ii) whether, prior to this decision, he or his Department engaged with young transgender people or organisations representing young transgender people; and (ii) whether an equality impact assessment has been undertaken in relation to this decision.
answer_text: (i) In April 2024, an Independent Review of gender identity services for children and young people (“the Cass Review”) found that there was not currently a reliable evidence-base upon which to make clinical decisions about the use of “puberty blockers” to treat gender dysphoria/incongruence, or for children and their families to make informed choices. The report concluded that because of the limited evidence, and potential risks to patient safety with regard to neurocognitive development, psychosexual development and longer-term bone health, these medicines should only be offered for this purpose under a research protocol.Following consideration of the responses received to a targeted UK-wide consultation, and as a consequence of recommendations made in a report to Ministers by the Commission for Human Medicines (CHM), the Department of Health, following Executive agreement, joined with the Department of Health and Social Care (DHSC) in England in the introduction of an indefinite UK-wide order to prevent new patients aged under the age of 18 from commencing to take gonadotrophin-releasing hormone (GnRH) analogues (“puberty blockers”) for the purposes of gender incongruence and/or gender dysphoria, under the care of private prescribers.This indefinite order replaced the UK’s previous emergency temporary restrictions on the use of GnRH analogues, which expired on 31 December 2024.  This indefinite ban came into operation from 1 January 2025 and will be subject to review in 2027.(ii) On 12 November and 9 December 2024, I met with representatives from local transgender representative organisations including the Rainbow Project, HereNI, Cara-Friend, Mermaids NI, the Belfast Trans Resource Centre as well as a number of service users and their parents regarding the ban and to outline my Department's plans for reforming gender affirming care services in Northern Ireland.My officials continue to meet with the Gender Identity Service Pathway Review Group and the Gender Identity Liaison Panel, which includes clinicians, transgender representative organisations, and service users to discuss the implications of the ban and seek their views. The reach of these groups and the service users is extensive, both regionally and nationally, and they actively liaise with other transgender groups.(iii) a UK-wide Equality Impact Assessment was produced and published following the completion of the UK-wide consultation on the proposal to introduce a permanent prohibition order limiting prescribing of puberty blockers. This can be viewed at:https://www.gov.uk/government/consultations/proposed-changes-to-the-availability-of-puberty-blockers-for-under-18s/equality-impact-assessment-proposed-changes-to-the-availability-of-puberty-blockers-consultation. The impact on those wishing to access puberty blockers and any associated anxiety about not being able to do so will be mitigated by the support being provided through the Child and Adolescent Mental Health Services (CAMHS) i

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail why the charges of tax evasion against the guilty parties were dropped in the Mobuoy illegal landfill prosecution.
answer_text: The Public Prosecution Service has responsibility for prosecutorial decisions including whether a defendant should be prosecuted and what charges they should face.   The Public Prosecution Service is operationally independent from my Department.  You may wish to contact the Public Prosecution Service directly for this information.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) for an update on operational preparations to introduce free hospital car parking in 2026; and (ii) for an estimated date for the implementation of free car parking.
answer_text: (i) Work is commencing at hospital sites across Northern Ireland in preparation for the removal of hospital car parking charges in 2026.Automatic Number Plate Recognition (ANPR) technology is being introduced to control parking, preserve blue light routes and protect designated spaces.Site location surveys across the Trusts have been undertaken by specialist engineers to prepare for the installation of ANPR cameras and associated engineering works. Installation of ANPR equipment commenced at Craigavon, Musgrave Park and Daisy Hill Hospitals in December 2024 with installation works to commence in Altnagelvin, Omagh, Ulster, Gransha and Mater hospitals during January 2025.A draft regional ANPR installation program, with a target completion date of June 2025, has been agreed with contractor.A press statement issued in November 2024 to advise members of the public that site work, to introduce the necessary infrastructure for a new traffic management system, is starting. Site work to enable free hospital car parking in 2026 | Department of Health(ii) In line with the Hospital Parking Charges Act 2024, free hospital car parking comes into operation on the 12 May 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice, in relation to her bid for funding to transform the criminal justice system, to detail the forms of technology that will be used, including the purpose of use.
answer_text: The ‘Speeding Up and Transforming the Criminal Justice System’ bid aims to transform criminal justice to reduce delay and maximise efficiencies. The transformation funding would be invested in reforming operational practice; making the best use of digital technology; and investment in evidence-based practice to inform key decisions.This proposal will seek to transform criminal justice by enhancing collaboration between police, prosecution, and the defence, and will divert, where appropriate, more low level cases away from courts, freeing up capacity within courts to deal with more serious cases, helping to reduce delay and pressure on the system.  The current bid focuses on two elements of the programme to speed up and transform the criminal justice system, looking at early engagement and non-court disposals.  There is potential for this work to be supported by digital technology and this will be part of key considerations as work progresses.Digital aspects of this work are at an early scoping stage and a successful bid would allow this work to be developed at greater pace.  Areas being explored include:development of new technology to enhance service delivery such as end-to-end digital casefiles to minimise cost of production and transport of vast amounts of paper between PPS and PSNI in the first instance;greater investment in data analytics, increasing understanding of patterns of need, promoting greater evidence-based practice and transparency;the use of technology to seek to streamline manual processes such as seeking to identify key evidence from lengthy digital evidence such as phones and Body Worn Video; andlooking at how to improve communication with victims and witnesses.My Department is responsible for the Causeway System, which is a strategically important criminal justice messaging system which allows for digital communication between criminal justice organisations, and provides the basis upon which many of these developments and improvements can be implemented.   Causeway has been pivotal in advancing the digital and reform agenda within the criminal justice sector and has been integral to the operation of the criminal justice system since its inception on 2004.  My Department has started the exercise of procuring a new system and this provides an opportunity to continue to develop the system to support the wider transformational change this bid can deliver.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for his assessment of the risk of the spread of avian influenza.
answer_text: The latest veterinary risk assessment conducted by my veterinary officials was completed in December 2024.  It is considered that the likelihood of a notifiable avian influenza incursion into poultry during the winter is LOW for flocks with high biosecurity and LOW (although the higher end of low) for free range flocks or those with sub-optimal biosecurity. The level of uncertainty is considered MEDIUM due to the shift in genotypes circulating in Europe and uncertainty regarding flock immunity in wild birds.The impact of an incursion of notifiable avian influenza to the poultry industry would be MODERATE.Overall, this means that the risk of notifiable avian influenza incursion into the Northern Ireland poultry flock is (the low end of) MODERATE with MEDIUM uncertainty. As of 12.00 noon on Saturday 18 January 2025, I took the decision to introduce an Avian Influenza Prevention Zone (AIPZ) in Northern Ireland following confirmation on Wednesday 15 January 2025 of HPAI H5N1 in a sampled collected from a wild goose found near Black Lough, Dungannon.  Furthermore, on Friday 17 January 2025 two further wild birds have tested positive for HPAI H5N1.  One was a buzzard found near Moira and the other a whooper swan in Portballintrae. The AIPZ places a legal requirement on all bird keepers in Northern Ireland to follow strict biosecurity measures.  This applies if you keep pet birds, commercial flocks or just a few birds in a backyard or hobby flock.  A copy of the AIPZ declaration which details the requirements that must be adhered to can be found on the DAERA website at https://www.daera-ni.gov.uk/publications/disease-control-zone-declarations
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of National Institute for Health and Care Excellence decisions his Department has appealed in each of the last five years.
answer_text: My Department established formal links with the National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE) on 1 July 2006 whereby guidance published by the Institute from that date would be locally reviewed for applicability to Northern Ireland and, where appropriate, endorsed for implementation in Health and Social Care (HSC). This link has ensured that Northern Ireland has access to up-to-date, independent, professional, evidence-based guidance on the value of health care interventions.Under the terms of this agreement, my Department does not reassess the clinical and cost evidence used by NICE in forming its advice. While my Department is entitled to participate in NICE’s Technology Appraisal and HST Programme as a commentator during the guidance development process, my Department does not have the right to appeal against any decision of the NICE Appraisal Committee.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of staff in (i) his Department; (ii) the Public Health Agency; and (iii) Health and Social Care Trusts that are working on future pandemic preparedness.
answer_text: Given the complexity of a pandemic response, planning involves staff  across a wide range of policy and service areas.  The number of staff engaged in this work will vary with time as staff may not necessarily work exclusively on pandemic preparedness.  It is therefore not possible to provide definitive staffing numbers.  However, I can advise that my Department chairs a Health and Social Care (HSC) Pandemic Resilience Board to coordinate planning across the HSC with representation from the HSC Strategic Planning and Performance Group, the Public Health Agency and the HSC Trusts.  A dedicated Pandemic Resilience Team within my Department, which currently has five staff members, oversees this work.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail (i) his plan to stop the spread of avian influenza; and (ii) whether he has brought these proposals to the Executive for discussion.
answer_text: I can advise that my Department is currently implementing a number of measures to ensure the poultry sector in Northern Ireland (NI) is prepared and protected against the potential of any highly pathogenic avian influenza (HPAI) incursion here.My officials have been undertaking a comprehensive communications campaign across DAERA social media channels and other external media to highlight the three key messages of reporting dead wild birds, registering kept birds and the utmost importance of biosecurity. The key messages have been disseminated to stakeholders, local councils and other public bodies including NICS departments.  Furthermore, detailed biosecurity guidance and a checklist has been provided on the DAERA website. My officials continue to work closely with their counterparts across the UK and the Republic of Ireland to monitor the situation to determine next steps as appropriate. The last case of avian influenza in kept birds in NI was confirmed during October 2022.  There has been one case of avian influenza in a wild bird confirmed this month, the last in NI occurring in September 2023.  I have not brought any proposals to the Executive for discussion at this stage.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

The AIPZ places a legal requirement on all bird keepers in Northern Ireland to follow strict biosecurity measures.  This applies if you keep pet birds, commercial flocks or just a few birds in a backyard or hobby flock. The last case of avian influenza in kept birds in NI was confirmed during October 2022.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for his assessment of whether property management companies are adequately regulated.
answer_text: I have made no assessment of whether property management companies are adequately regulated, as this matter falls outside the remit of my Department and is the responsibility of the Minister of Finance.   I have however recently agreed that my officials liaise with counterparts in DoF to inform their work on the potential regulation of this sector.  It is my understanding that this work is at the very early scoping stage, with discussions planned with my officials on its parameters in the coming months.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the number of children living in absolute and relative poverty after housing costs, broken down by constituency.
answer_text: Official measures of absolute and relative poverty for NI are derived from the Family Resources Survey (FRS). Due to the uncertainty around estimates at lower levels, the Department only publishes poverty rates at local council area level for the whole NI population, and not specifically for children. The Department does not publish poverty rates at a constituency level.Further information regarding the Family Resources Survey and NI poverty statistics can be found at the link below.https://www.communities-ni.gov.uk/topics/family-resources-survey-and-poverty-analysisThe Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) annually release official statistics for “Children in low-income families: local area statistics”. This includes the number of children in each constituency in NI estimated to be living in poverty before housing costs but not after housing costs.Further information regarding the Children in Low Income Families (CILIF) statistics can be found at the link below.www.gov.uk/government/collections/children-in-low-income-families-local-area-statistics

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any (i) plans; and (ii) work undertaken to purchase dilapidated properties for the purpose of providing temporary accommodation.
answer_text: I have no plans in this regard at this time.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether her Department will consider ending the industrial derating policy.
answer_text: There are no plans to end the industrial derating policy at this time. We have a strong manufacturing sector that supports a significant number of jobs, any policy decisions need to align with our economic vision of good jobs, improving productivity, decarbonisation and regional balance.  I have laid out my roadmap and forward approach for rating policy in my Statement to the Assembly on 9 December.  Minister of Finance Oral Statement - Rating Policy - Strategic Roadmap

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for his assessment of whether local councils are adequately resourced to use enforcement powers against landlords who fail to fulfil their obligations to tenants.
answer_text: Local councils are responsible for the allocation of their budgets, adequately resourcing enforcement and ensuring that enforcement officers are adequately qualified, trained, experienced and competent to carry out their duties.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether she has considered using income, rather than property values, as a proxy for ability to pay when calculating domestic rates liability.
answer_text: Domestic rating is a property tax, assessed on property values, known within the tax as a “rateable capital values”. Means-tested support exists within the system in the form of rate rebate, low income rate relief scheme and Housing Benefit for rates. Income tax remains a reserved matter at Westminster in the context of the devolution settlement.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for her assessment of whether current public procurement policy permits public bodies to trade in goods and services produced in areas that are illegally occupied, in violation of international law.
answer_text: Public procurement policy covers the procurement of goods, services and works. It does not cover trade in goods and services.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 18244/22-27, and in the absence of plans for enforcement, to detail any steps he is taking to protect tenants who are issued with an eviction notice after challenging a second rent increase in a 12-month period.
answer_text: When my Department commences this legislation, tenants will be under no obligation to pay any second rent increase within a 12-month period as this will be illegal.   The landlord cannot evict without following due process in law. This includes the tenant being served with the correct notice to quit, and my Department is developing regulations that will lengthen these notice periods considerably. It may also require the landlord to apply to the Court to commence ejectment proceedings. As previously advised the implementation of the restriction will be underpinned by a comprehensive awareness strategy with the aim of ensuring that tenants, landlords and their representatives are aware of their respective rights and responsibilities in relation to these issues. It will signpost to other sources of advice and guidance, including to Housing Rights, which continues to be funded by my Department to provide a broad range of services to tenants, such as, advocacy and mediation.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance how the £235 million Transformation Fund will be divided amongst public services.
answer_text: ANSWERThe £235m Transformation Fund is over a five-year period. I will be tabling my recommendations to the Executive shortly on transformation proposals which have progressed to the second stage of the assessment process, so that important funding decisions can be made on these proposals. I expect further advice from the interim Board in relation to digital transformation proposals in the coming months and, depending on the level of remaining funding, I anticipate a further call for transformation proposals thereafter.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether a person applying for payment through the Historical Institutional Abuse redress board can pass any successful payment when completed onto family members, other than a spouse, if they pass away before their application is completed.
answer_text: If an applicant to the Redress Board dies after an application for compensation has been received, but before it has been determined, then that application can only be progressed by the deceased’s surviving spouse, civil partner or cohabiting partner, if that person is a residuary beneficiary of the deceased’s estate; or any surviving child of the deceased, if that child is a residuary beneficiary of the deceased’s estate. This is in accordance with Section 6(1) of the Historical Institutional Abuse (Northern Ireland) Act 2019 and Rule 5 of the Historical Institutional Abuse Redress Board (Applications and Appeals) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2020.There is also provision under Section 6(2) of the Act for surviving spouse, civil partner, cohabiting partner or surviving child of a deceased who is a beneficiary of the deceased’s estate, but not a residuary beneficiary to make an application and receive payment if a Redress Board panel considers that compensation should be paid, and it would be in the interest of justice to do so. Any award for compensation made by a panel in respect of a deceased can only be paid to individual(s) in accordance with provisions of the deceased’s Last Will & Testament in accordance with Section 12(5) of the Historical Institutional Abuse (Northern Ireland) Act 2019 or if the deceased person died intestate any compensation awarded by a panel can only be paid in accordance with the laws of intestacy in the country in which the deceased was resident at the time of death in accordance with Section 6(6) of the Historical Institutional Abuse (Northern Ireland) Act 2019.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on negotiations with Treasury in relation to changes to the Housing Executive’s borrowing powers, in order to enable the construction of more social homes.
answer_text: I raised the matter with the Chief Secretary to the Treasury at a meeting on the 3rd October 2024 and immediately followed this with a written request for him to consider the matter. Included with this letter was a paper setting out the case for a change in the treatment of Housing Executive borrowing, which had been produced by the Department for Communities. My Department is continuing to press Treasury for a response.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail whether the Cost of Doing Business Research will include a critical examination of rates relief offered to multinational corporations, including industrial derating.
answer_text: The Terms of Reference for the Cost of Doing Business research can be found on the Department’s website - Cost of Doing Business Research - Terms of Reference.
