question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for his assessment of the Northern Ireland Functional Assessment Service contract being awarded to Capita, in light of concerns raised about the organisation and its assessment practices.
answer_text: The Functional Assessment Service procurement was conducted by the Department for Work and Pensions in line with the Public Contracts Regulations 2015 ensuring a legally compliant, fair and competitive tender process.  Bids were evaluated and scored against a number of criteria including quality, price, and social value. Capita achieved the highest score in Lot 5 (Northern Ireland) and were declared the successful bidder.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 16391/22-27, (i) why the level of recruitment agency staff employed by his Department increased by 54 per cent between 2023 and 2024; and (ii) why these workers were not directly employed by his Department.
answer_text: The level of recruitment of agency staff employed by the Department for Communities between 2023 and 2024 has increased due to an expansion in service delivery for the Department for Work and Pensions. Lack of supply of substantive Administrative Officers at that time resulted in positions being filled using the Department of Finance contract for supply of temporary agency workers.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when the Housing Executive will commission work to agree a temporary accommodation standards framework.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive.  I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“The Housing Executive has commenced the development of a temporary accommodation standards framework which will include physical, suitability and service standards. The framework is in the early stages of development and further updates will be available in due course.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the NIHE directly.  Making direct contact with the ALB will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for her assessment of the policing operation used at the Palestine protest at Queen's University Belfast on Thursday 14 November 2024.
answer_text: The policing of demonstrations is an operational matter for the PSNI who is accountable to the Northern Ireland Policing Board. As I am committed to respecting the operational independence of the PSNI and the Policing Board, it would not be appropriate for me to comment.  You may, therefore, wish to raise this matter directly with the Chief Constable.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities (i) how the £17 million allocated in the October Monitoring Round will be used to support those affected by the withdrawal of the Winter Fuel Payment; and (ii) whether this support will be means-tested.
answer_text: I refer to my statement in the Assembly on 19 November in relation to a one-off fuel payment to eligible households.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the actions he is taking to reduce spend on temporary accommodation and towards the prevention of homelessness.
answer_text: I am putting in place a number of policies to increase Housing Supply to reduce pressure on temporary accommodation spend as outlined in the Housing Supply Strategy.In addition, the Housing Executive has initiated a Housing Solutions Task and Finish Group which is making progress looking at options for alternative and more financially sustainable options for temporary accommodation.The Housing Executive’s strategic approach to reducing spend on temporary accommodation and towards the prevention of homelessness is outlined in our Strategic Action Plan for Temporary Accommodation and Homelessness Strategy 2022-27. The Housing Executive develops annual action plans to support the delivery of both documents.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he consulted with (i) trade unions; and (ii) health service workers in relation to his Department’s plans for a bodycam pilot programme in the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: The Belfast Health and Social Care Trust has a Steering Group to oversee the development of a policy and the planning for a pilot to use body worn cameras.  Trade Unions are members of the Steering Group and will contribute to the policy draft.Once drafted, the policy will be subject to extensive engagement with Trade Unions, Belfast Trust staff and some relevant organisations before it is finalised.  The policy will then go to a full public consultation.  The Trust will take cognisance of the feedback received to inform a decision to start a pilot.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities how the £24 million allocated in the October Monitoring Round will be enough to build up to 800 social homes, when the £20 million allocated during the June Monitoring Round funded the construction of up to 200 social homes.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive.I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“The additionality of 200 new social housing starts arising from the June monitoring round allocation of £20m was a Departmental estimate based on the average Housing Association Grant (HAG) per unit. However, funding for most new social housing schemes is accrued over the lifetime of the construction period i.e. not all of the HAG per unit is accrued in the current programme year.Utilisation of the Social Housing Development Programme (SHDP) budget to deliver new social housing starts is also dependent on several other factors, including the timing of start on-site (i.e. early SHDP starts will generate higher proportionate ‘in-year’ spend). Initial budget/unit projections therefore also factored in the likelihood of earlier SHDP Starts, whereas further funding confirmed subsequently will be allocated against schemes starting later in the programme year. In addition to the above factors, an element of the £20m from the June Monitoring Round was required to be earmarked for other SHDP-related expenditure (e.g. Disability Adaptations Grant), whereas the £24m is expected to be fully allocated to the delivery of new social housing”. The Housing Executive is currently assessing what can be delivered with this additional funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 17407/22-27, to detail (i) the two sites referenced; and (ii) the extent of the work carried out in relation to relocating Gaelscoil an Lonnáin to the sites.
answer_text: The two sites identified were a public sector asset located on Falls Road and a site on Distillery Street. My department had commenced initial investigations on the suitability of each of the sites in question. However as outlined in my previous responses to AQW 17405/22-27, AQW 17407/22-27 and AQW 17849/22-27, this work is now paused as pre-publication consultation is ongoing on a proposal by the Board of Governors to discontinue the school with effect from 31 August 2025, or as soon as possible thereafter.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, in order to address housing waiting lists, whether he has considered introducing a pathway scheme for people who live in houses that are too big for their current situation and would like to downsize.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive.  I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“The situation where a current social housing tenant wishes to move to other accommodation is dealt with through the transfer process.  The provisions within the Housing Selection Scheme around transfers are designed, in part, to allow landlords to make the best use of social housing stock.  Tenants wishing to downsize are also advised of the (mutual) exchange process. Proposal 6 of the ongoing Fundamental Review of Allocations is designed to achieve greater use of the mutual exchange service through raising customer awareness and better management information for housing professionals.  This proposal is in the process of being implemented now.  There are currently no plans for additional measures beyond those outlined.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Northern Ireland Housing Executive directly. Making direct contact with the Northern Ireland Housing Executive will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, in advance of the outcome of the current review of welfare mitigations, to detail any preparations his Department is making for the extension of the benefit cap mitigation beyond 31 March 2025.
answer_text: I refer the member to the statement I made on 5 December.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the number of responses his Department received for each public consultation related to (i) the Private Tenancies Act (Northern Ireland) 2022; and (ii) the outworking, including regulations, of the Private Tenancies Act (Northern Ireland) 2022.
answer_text: The Private Tenancies Act (Northern Ireland) 2022 was informed by the 'Proposals for Change’ consultation exercise, which was published in 2017. My Department’s consultation on these proposals received 52 responses. The draft Private Tenancies Bill was then subject to targeted consultation with key stakeholders, which received a further 13 responses. On the outworkings, including regulations, of the Private Tenancies Act (Northern Ireland) 2022, my Department has received a total of 619 responses to date.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities how the provision in the Private Tenancies Act (Northern Ireland) 2022 to limit rent increases to a maximum of once every 12 months will be monitored and enforced.
answer_text: If a landlord attempts to increase the rent more than once in every 12-month period or does not give the right notice period, then the landlord’s action has no legal effect and the rent remains unchanged. A landlord cannot enforce the increase in court, nor can they harass the tenant.Compliance with the restriction on rent increases will be underpinned by a comprehensive engagement strategy undertaken by my officials. This strategy will be aimed at informing landlords and tenants, representative groups and wider stakeholders of the legislative change. It will also advise landlords and tenants where further advice and guidance can be found on this and other key aspects of PRS legislation.  My Department will monitor any concerns post implementation of the restriction by liaising with councils and key stakeholders, such as, Housing Rights.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any plans that exist to use the bog meadows in West Belfast for water capture and flood prevention purposes.
answer_text: The Living With Water in Belfast Plan, published in November 2021, aims to reduce flood risk, whilst improving the water quality in our rivers and enabling economic development.  Through this NI Executive endorsement Plan, my Department has identified a number of locations throughout the greater Belfast area, where opportunities possibly exist to use green spaces and open areas near rivers to store water and help reduce flood risk.Bog Meadows is one of the opportunity areas identified and whilst there are no specific projects currently planned, my Department will continue to work with partners to develop any opportunities in the area to reduce the risk of flooding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for his assessment of the UK Government’s broader definition of public debt; and how this will impact on the Housing Executive’s ability to invest in the construction of social homes.
answer_text: It is a matter for the UK Government to define public debt and how it is assessed and measured.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the number of people living in West Belfast that have had their application for a tenancy succession rejected by the Housing Executive in the last five years, broken down by (i) legal succession rejections; and (ii) policy succession rejections.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive. I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows.“The Housing Executive does not hold or collate the information in the format requested.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Northern Ireland Housing Executive directly. Making direct contact with the Northern Ireland Housing Executive will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any plans to ensure an adequate standard of living and the right to housing for young people leaving the care system.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive.  I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“The Housing Executive’s Youth Homelessness Action Plan launched on 25th November 2024, contains a commitment to review the Regional Good Practice Guidance to ensure that young people leaving care are supported. The Housing Executive has also committed to the introduction of dedicated Housing Advisors/Team in relation to young people presenting as homeless and has facilitated training with the Children’s Law Centre for these staff, who work closely in partnership with the relevant Health and Social Care trust, shaping the service around the needs of the young person. Local Planning Partnership Groups have also been established in each trust to address need and that seek to improve outcomes for young people.The Supporting People Programme delivers housing related support for young people, including those who have cared experience, to live independently in the community.  This is tailored to meet their needs through accommodation-based provision for those in housing need.  Floating support services help young people with a new tenancy/set up for a new home and to help prevent homelessness.The Housing Executive’s support for young people leaving care is set out in legislation and outlined within the NIHE / HSC Regional Good Practice Guidance on Meeting the Accommodation and Support Needs of Young People aged 16-21.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the NIHE directly.  Making direct contact with the ALB will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance (i) why INSPIRE Index Polygons for Northern Ireland have not been publicly released, having been mandated under European law pre-Brexit; and (ii) when he will commit to publishing them.
answer_text: Department of Finance have made the assumption that INSPIRE Index Polygons are INSPIRE Cadastral Parcels (https://knowledge-base.inspire.ec.europa.eu/cadastral-parcels_en).Land & Property Services (LPS) has previously had this data publicly released on the Spatial NI for INSPIRE portal from 2013.  This service has been temporarily withdrawn from public access since May 2023 due to security issues with the Spatial NI for INSPIRE infrastructure.  LPS has also been awaiting clarification from the lead department Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs (DEFRA) if the UK will request any changes to INSPIRE data post Brexit.  The data is still made available by LPS under the terms and conditions of the INSPIRE licence to anyone who requests it.  The licence states that the data can only be used “for the purposes of public tasks that may have an impact on the environment”.  There have been no more than 10 requests for the data since May 2023.To ensure good use of public funds and to deliver value for money, LPS is awaiting direction from DEFRA on the future plans for INSPIRE before making a decision on any new methods of providing INSPIRE data as this would involve investing heavily in a new platform to make the data publicly available.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, in light of his Department’s introduction of the new Planning Portal, (i) whether the Gunning principles on public consultation apply to planning applications; and (ii) whether these principles informed the design of the new online system.
answer_text: The Gunning Principles relate to public consultations and originate from cases involving, for example, policy development and the making of decisions between alternative policy proposals.In the planning field, statute often specifies procedural requirements which consultation must achieve. The duty to consult on planning applications remains subject to the established principles as set out by Sedley J in Gunning. The portal was designed to facilitate such consultation.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department collects data in relation to the proportion of patients or health service users in (i) primary care settings; and (ii) secondary care settings whose first language is not English.
answer_text: My Department does not collect data in relation to the proportion of patients or health service users in primary care settings whose first language is not English.None of the five Health and Social Care Trusts in Northern Ireland collect data in relation to the proportion of patients or health service users in Trust-provided settings whose first language is not English.Foreign language interpreting support for health and social care providers in Northern Ireland is available through the Business Services Organisation (BSO) Interpreting Service, commissioned by the Department of Health. The service aims to improve access to HSC services for patients who do not speak English proficiently.The Department of Health has a contract to provide a telephone interpreting service for all Family Practitioner Services contractors.  Family Practitioner Services contractors can also avail of a translation service for translating medical records into English. This can be accessed through their Practice Support Manager in the Department’s Strategic Planning and Performance Group (SPPG).

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department has considered developing a flood alleviation scheme for the St. James area of West Belfast.
answer_text: As part of the Northern Ireland Flood Risk Assessment 2018, Belfast was identified as an Area of Potential Significant Flood Risk. The publicly available 2021–2027 – Second Cycle Northern Ireland Flood Risk Management Plan provides further detail on the flood risk in Belfast and includes a summary of flooding history.My Department has no works programmed for flood alleviation schemes in the St. James area of West Belfast and are not aware of any flood related issues. It has been over 30 years since a call was received to report flooding in this area. If there is some particular concern regarding this area you wish to raise, please do so by contacting the Greater Belfast office directly on 028 9260 6100 or via email: rivers.belfast@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the number of historic flood zones that exist in West Belfast.
answer_text: My Department’s flood maps (Flood Maps NI | Department for Infrastructure) highlight the areas  that are considered to be at flood risk.As part of the Flood Risk Assessment 2018, Belfast was identified as an Area of Potential Significant Flood Risk. The publicly available 2021–2027 – Second Cycle Northern Ireland Flood Risk Management Plan provides further detail on the flood risk in Belfast and includes a summary of flooding history.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the amount that developers have paid towards (i) roads; and (ii) other infrastructure costs in each of the last ten years.
answer_text: My Department does not retain information directly related to the amount developers have paid towards roads and infrastructure costs.However, my Department does have records dating back to 2018 of the value of bonds taken out for roads infrastructure within new developments as shown below.  YearTotal value of bonds held2018/19£90,373,1562019/20£75,564,9522020/21£63,128,1002021/22£68,129,5632022/23£43,742,1042023/24£69,302,4002024/25 to date£46,745,204My Department also has records of the value of monies paid by developers and other bodies, to complete improvements to road infrastructure.  YearDeveloper's Contributions2018/19£874,0002019/20£1,219,0002020/21£911,0002021/22£500,0002022/23£483,0002023/24£359,000

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 17237/22-27, for his assessment of whether the reconfiguration of maternity services has an impact on instances of births before arrival at hospital.
answer_text: Since the amalgamation of inpatient maternity services and births in the Northern health and Social Care Trust on 17 July 2023, to date 20 women have experienced birth before arrival to hospital, four of whom were women who reside within the Causeway locality. I have been advised that Trusts do not hold specific information in relation to the location of the birth, or pertaining to birthing in a car or ambulance and that our systems don’t currently collate information on the clinical reasons for, or circumstances behind, birth before arrival at hospital. My officials will continue to monitor births before arrival to hospitals to identify if there has been any increase as a result of the reconfiguration of maternity services in the Northern Trust locality.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the establishment of the mother and baby unit at Belfast City Hospital.
answer_text: The Belfast Health and Social Care Trust has now completed a draft business case for the provision of the proposed regional unit at Belfast City Hospital site.The business case for the Mother and Baby Unit is currently with SPPG as the commissioner for consideration. However, progression of this project will remain subject to the required capital and revenue funding being identified.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he plans to introduce a regional screening programme for prostate cancer.
answer_text: The UK National Screening Committee (UK NSC), an independent scientific committee which advises the four nations, does not currently recommend screening for prostate cancer.This is because the Prostate Specific Antigen (PSA) test is not accurate enough to detect prostate cancer that needs treatment. It is currently unclear if other tests such as a magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) scan, with or without PSA, are accurate enough.Research is looking at whether a method for predicting prostate cancer risk using a combination of a blood test and other information about a man could be more accurate. However, more studies are necessary to confirm the early results.My department will continue to be guided by the UK NSC’s recommendations on proposals for screening programmes.Anyone with symptoms which they believe may be related to the prostate gland should contact their GP.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 17237/22-27, whether his Department collects information on the clinical reasons for, or circumstances behind, birth before arrival at hospital.
answer_text: This information is not readily available.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for a breakdown of the number of households on the social housing waiting list for each Common Landlord Area in West Belfast in the last five years, broken down by (i) those with Full Duty Applicant status; and (ii) those in housing stress.
answer_text: Due to the volume of detail required to answer AQW 18030/22-27, the information has been placed in the Assembly Library.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any consideration they have given to including death, dying, and bereavement as a key priority in the final Programme for Government.
answer_text: We refer the member to the answer given to AQW 16980/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) whether construction of Grand Central Station is over-budget; and (ii) for a breakdown of costs incurred to date.
answer_text: The full business case for the Belfast Transport Hub was approved in 2021 at a total cost of £294.6m. The expenditure to date is £280.4m and the revised estimated project cost is £344.5m. The majority of this cost increase is due to the extraordinary inflation that has impacted on all major capital projects. The costs incurred to date are as follows.ElementLetter of Offer BudgetProfessional and management Fees£11,382,115Statutory Fess & Surveys£310,960Land£710,732Enabling Works£2,799,855Main works £142,140,731Railway Signalling & Telecommunications£68,878,203Preparatory Ground Works£53,260,000Road works£948,431TOTAL£280,431,027A revised Final Business Case is currently being considered by my Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the width of (i) the new rail platforms at Belfast Grand Central Station; and (ii) the rail platforms at the now closed Great Victoria Street Station.
answer_text: The new rail platforms at Belfast Grand Central Station are approximately 6m wide.  The rail platforms at the now closed Great Victoria Street Station were approximately 4.2m wide

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) the reason for the delay in the implementation of the Pay Review Body's recommendation for healthcare workers; and (ii) when this will be implemented.
answer_text: Unfortunately, the NI budgetary position means that I am not in a position to deliver the pay settlements that I would like for this year.Ahead of this week’s NI Executive monitoring round, my Department had identified remaining financial pressures totalling £450m. This total included £320m to implement the pay increases recommended by health service pay review bodies. Alongside these pay pressures, and despite our Health Service identifying an unprecedented level of savings of £200m, there remains a £130m budget deficit, representing the gap between available funding and the cost of maintaining core health and social care services at current levels.The monitoring round allocated an additional £350m for Health. While a significant and welcome sum, this still leaves a funding gap of £100m. This remaining gap means that I am not currently in a position to match, in full, the pay awards made to health staff in England and Wales for this year.I am deeply dissatisfied with this position and recognise the justifiable frustration that the HSC workforce will feel. I have sought an early meeting with health service trade unions to discuss next steps on pay offers for 2024/25.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, given the unresolved issue of remediation of the Mobuoy site, whether his Department is considering alternative routes for the unbuilt section of the A6 between Drumahoe and Maydown.
answer_text: As you will be aware, part of the road alignment for Phase 2 of the A6 encroaches on the Mobuoy waste site; once plans for remediation of that site are in place, a delivery strategy for Phase 2 including timelines will be finalised. My Department continues to work collaboratively with the NIEA and other stakeholders, to progress the remediation of this site. This integrated approach will ensure that any proposals take account of each parties’ requirements, including those associated with the A6.My officials are currently waiting on DAERA to confirm their Optimum Remediation Strategy and provide a programme for the remediation of the Mobuoy site.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure how the needs of community transport service users and the wider community are considered in the work of the cross-departmental working group on community transport.
answer_text: I recently met with Minister Nesbitt (DoH) and Minister Muir (DAERA) on 9 September 2024 as part of the Cross Departmental Working Group, to explore what options are there to best meet the needs of users and the potential for community transport to support the work and objectives of other departments.  My officials are continuing to engage with colleagues in DoH and DAERA to explore potential options for community transport to support other departments.  This work will consider the needs of all stakeholders.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education (i) for his assessment of the possible closure of Gaelscoil an Lonnáin; and (ii) to detail any work his Department has undertaken to seek another location for the school.
answer_text: I refer the Member to my previous responses to AQW 17405/22-27 and AQW 17407/22-27. It is for the managing authority of Gaelscoil an Lonnáin to bring forward any proposals in relation to the future of the school. Should a Development Proposal be brought forward, I will make a decision based on the evidence presented.  My department only becomes engaged in the area-planning process when a Development Proposal is published by the Education Authority.My department had explored two potential sites for the relocation of Gaelscoil an Lonnáin, however, this work is now paused as pre-publication consultation is ongoing on a proposal by the Board of Governors to discontinue the school with effect from 31 August 2025, or as soon as possible thereafter.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail (i) the number of Heritage at Risk buildings; (ii) any work he has undertaken to support these buildings; and (iii) any assessment that has been made of the possibility of utilising a portion of these properties for housing.
answer_text: On 31 March 2023, the last date for which official statistics were published on this issue, there were 1,037 buildings and structures on the Heritage at Risk Northern Ireland (HARNI) database. The vast majority of these are buildings.Heritage at Risk (HAR) are one of three priority categories supported for repairs under the department’s Historic Environment Fund. This is currently limited to £6k per application.  The department also supports the Architectural Heritage Fund (AHF) to work with communities to develop solutions to HAR, and, in partnership with DAERA, NIHE and AHF to deliver capital support of up to £200k for HAR in villages.A number of these buildings have been reused for housing. My department is considering supporting research into this issue in the coming year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any efforts to work with the Minister for Communities to increase investment in the arts.
answer_text: Funding for the Arts is provided mainly through the Arts Council who are an arms-length body of the Department for Communities. My 2024-25 Budget and in-year monitoring proposals which were accepted by the Executive have provided the Minister for Communities with some £877.5m of Resource funding for the 2024/25 financial year compared to £861m in the previous year. This included a specific allocation in June Monitoring of £0.5m, ring-fenced for the Arts. I fully support investment in the Arts, but is ultimately a matter for the Communities Minister to determine the level of funding for the Arts from his allocated budget.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) his legislative proposals for sustainable urban drainage; and (ii) an indicative time frame for introducing this legislation.
answer_text: (i) My proposals for a Water, Flooding and Sustainable Drainage Bill will provide enabling powers to make regulations for sustainable drainage (SuDS).  The regulations will make provision for SuDS guidance, as well as establishing a SuDS approval body to assess SuDS proposals, if required, and will set out requirements to ensure the long-term maintenance of SuDS. This will encourage developers to use SuDS as the preferred drainage solution in new developments.(ii) I am planning to introduce the Water, Flooding and Sustainable Drainage Bill in early 2025, subject to the approval of Executive colleagues.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether the decision to appeal the Court of Appeal’s judgment on the No Gas Caverns ruling to the Supreme Court was his decision or taken by the Executive.
answer_text: As set out in my statement to the Assembly on 9 July 2024, although the decision was mine, I sought the views of Executive colleagues before making the decision to appeal.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether the review of his Department’s Memorandum of Understanding with the Ulster Farmers' Union will include a reassessment of the commitment for the NIEA to give 14 days’ notice of a farmyard inspection.
answer_text: As previously signalled, I have asked the Northern Ireland Environment Agency to review the use of the Memorandum of Understanding that it agreed with the Ulster Farmers' Union in 2017 with a view to replacing it with a statement of principles that will set out how the agency will engage with all those that it regulates and inspects.  Once I receive proposals from the agency I will consider the way forward.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he will publish a consultation document on how co-production will be used in his Department's plans for transformation and reconfiguration.
answer_text: The HSC Reform Act 2009 places a statutory duty on certain HSC bodies, including the HSC Trusts, to involve service users and carers in the planning and delivery of services. The Department’s guidance on the change or withdrawal of services reinforces the importance of involving those who use the services in the decision-making process. Indeed, this was also an important part of the ‘Delivering Together 2026’ plan.My Department and the HSC bodies will continue to comply with the existing legislation and guidance as reform and reconfiguration plans are developed. I do not have any plans to publish a consultation document on this matter.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, pursuant to AQW 16526/22-27, for his assessment of whether the actual emissions of greenhouse gases from landfills, including illegal dumps, will be significantly higher than the estimated 580.1 kilotonnes as outlined in his answer.
answer_text: Waste buried in shallow illegal dumps does not typically lead to the anoxic conditions that predicate the decay of organic matter to generate a high level of methane, the main greenhouse gas associated with waste dump sites. Therefore, it is not anticipated that greenhouse gas emissions from illegal dump sites will add significantly to the estimated 580.1 Kilotonnes outlined in the previous answer however, as set out in the GHG inventory, there is a level of uncertainty associated with all GHG estimates.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether they plan to correspond with the Prime Minister over a joint response to recent far-right violence and efforts to safeguard minorities.
answer_text: Racial Equality is a devolved matter and therefore we remain dedicated to ensuring that we as an Executive deliver the commitments in the current Racial Equality Strategy 2015-2025, and engage with relevant bodies to address the race hate incidents strategically. This will continue to be a priority for us.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether there is a commitment to increase expenditure on public transport.
answer_text: The funding of an effective public transport service is a key priority for my Department.  However, it has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government.  The All-Island Strategic Rail Review, which was published in July seeks to address the limitations of the existing network by proposing additional track capacity, electrification, increased speeds, higher service frequencies and new routes.  Extensive capital spend will be required to fulfil the Review’s recommendations in the coming years.Further expenditure has been committed to the Enterprise Fleet Replacement Programme which will see the replacement of the existing fleet with modern train sets with significantly reduced emissions.  Consequently, a more reliable, more frequent and faster journey (under 2 hours) from 2029 onwards will be delivered.  The programme will be jointly funded by the Department of Infrastructure (DfI) and the Department of Transport (DoT) as well as funding from the Peace Plus programme, managed by the Special EU Programme Body (SEUPB).In the meantime, the value of our public transport services cannot be overstated, and I will continue to work with Executive colleagues to ensure appropriate investment is made.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail why the Department for Justice's request for additional resource through the transformation bid for the Ending Violence Against Women and Girls Strategy was not agreed.
answer_text: Ending Violence Against Women and Girls is of paramount importance to me and indeed the Executive as a whole, and that is why it is a priority in the draft Programme for Government.While there are many valuable proposals which have been put forward to the interim Public Sector Transformation Board, unfortunately there is finite funding available, with requests for funding some three times greater than level of funding available across the five-year period. The focus of the interim Board is to identify those proposals that offer the greatest potential to transform public services – be that doing things differently to deliver better outcomes, or more cost effectively. This approach is in keeping with what was agreed by the Executive on 9 May. Where proposals are unsuccessful feedback will be provided to help departments strengthen proposals for future rounds of funding, or departments can fund them from within their own resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQT 656/22-27 answered during Question Time on 15 October 2024, (i) to detail where the £200 million of savings have been made across the Health and Social Care Trusts; and (ii) for his assessment of the impact of these savings on patient care and safety.
answer_text: Challenging savings targets of over £200m have been set across the Department and its Arm’s Length bodies. Of the £200m target HSC Trusts have been apportioned targets of £192m.  The breakdown of these savings is detailed in the following table.Savings measures taken forward by Trusts£mWorkforce87.2 Community care provision efficiencies17.0 Other service reconfigurations and efficiencies 11.2 Corporate and other efficiencies / targeted expenditure reductions 48.2 Stretch targets for other savings by Trusts18.5 Medicines Optimisation Regional Efficiencies10.0Total192.1Whilst I recognise that the financial situation is exerting significant pressure on all services and these savings will undoubtedly have some impact on service delivery they will not have the sorts of impact that I would regard as catastrophic to the functioning of our Health and Social Care system.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail her plans to collect and publish more up-to-date data on deprivation.
answer_text: I refer the member to the response issued for AQW 9329 22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he plans to update guidance on the transformation and reconfiguration of services to ensure that co-production is mandatory.
answer_text: The HSC Reform Act 2009 places a statutory duty on certain HSC bodies, including the HSC Trusts, to involve service users and carers in the planning and delivery of services. The Department’s guidance on the change or withdrawal of services reinforces the importance of involving those who use the services in the decision-making process. Indeed, this was also an important part of the ‘Delivering Together 2026’ plan.The legislation and existing guidance applies to plans when considering and implementing service changes and reconfigurations.  I currently have no plans to amend the extant guidance.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 15902/22-27, whether the evidence considered by the Permanent Secretary will be published.
answer_text: The evidence considered by the Permanent Secretary is available at https://www.health-ni.gov.uk/sites/default/files/publications/health/doh-foi-0054-2024.pdf

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for his assessment of the cost to bring the current Gaelscoil an Lonnáin site to a suitable standard.
answer_text: Given the restricted site and current configuration, it is not practical to bring the current Gaelscoil an Lonnáin site up to the standard of a new school build. In the constrained capital budget position, my Department’s priority is to ensure that schools stay open and that pupils are safe. In this context, it recently progressed two schemes at the school, at a total value of £270,000, to address health and safety concerns and to improve the outdoor play area. The Department of Education had explored two potential sites for the relocation of Gaelscoil an Lonnáin, however, this work is now paused as pre-publication consultation is ongoing on a proposal by the Board of Governors to discontinue Gaelscoil an Lonnáin with effect from 31 August 2025, or as soon as possible thereafter.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the groups and organisations he has consulted with in relation to the future of Gaelscoil an Lonnáin.
answer_text: It is for the managing authority of Gaelscoil an Lonnáin to bring forward any proposals in relation to the future of the school. Should a Development Proposal be brought forward, I will make a decision based on the evidence presented.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the work carried out to ensure his Department’s estate can adequately accommodate a 40 per cent return to office work pattern.
answer_text: Most of my department’s estate remains unchanged since before the Covid-19 pandemic and is more than sufficient to accommodate a hybrid 40% office attendance.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any work his Department has undertaken to identify a new site for Gaelscoil an Lonnáin.
answer_text: Given the restricted site and current configuration, it is not practical to bring the current Gaelscoil an Lonnáin site up to the standard of a new school build. In the constrained capital budget position, my Department’s priority is to ensure that schools stay open and that pupils are safe. In this context, it recently progressed two schemes at the school, at a total value of £270,000, to address health and safety concerns and to improve the outdoor play area. The Department of Education had explored two potential sites for the relocation of Gaelscoil an Lonnáin, however, this work is now paused as pre-publication consultation is ongoing on a proposal by the Board of Governors to discontinue Gaelscoil an Lonnáin with effect from 31 August 2025, or as soon as possible thereafter.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether she has raised the issue of the British Government’s representation on the permanent Public Sector Transformation Board with the (i) Secretary of State; and (ii) First Minister and deputy First Minister.
answer_text: Establishing a Public Sector Transformation Board was a requirement to access the £235m of ringfenced funding contained in the financial package. The role and composition of the Board in this regard has been set out a number of times, including in the financial package itself; correspondence from the Chief Secretary to the Treasury; and the Interim Fiscal Framework.The Executive, including the First and deputy First Ministers, agreed my proposal for establishing the Transformation Programme on 9 May. This proposal referenced the requirement for a British Government representative on the interim Board, and I corresponded with the then Secretary of State to confirm his nominee in that regard. I was also clear in that correspondence that this important work must respect devolved decision-making on transformation. That is the approach being taken.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline his Department’s work to ensure workers receive all of their tips.
answer_text: Question 15 – AQO 11323/22-27My ‘Good Jobs’ Employment Rights Bill consultation included proposals to introduce legislation to ensure tips left to workers are passed on to them in full.  The consultation closed on 30 September and my Department is analysing responses to inform next steps.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when the Department’s commissioned research on the Housing First approach to ending homelessness will be published.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive.  I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“The Housing Executive has commissioned independent research on Housing First that has been supported by a range of statutory and voluntary partners as part of a Research Advisory Group. As part of the work of the Research Advisory Group, a draft report has been shared with members for review / comment and the report will now be subject to Housing Executive approval process with an expected publication date of late 2024 early 2025.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the NIHE directly.  Making direct contact with the ALB will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when the Department’s commissioned research on intimidation points will be published.
answer_text: The research report will be published, by the Housing Executive, in due course following completion of the policy development process.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the amount of (i) grants; and (ii) other financial assistance that has been provided to Moy Park by Invest NI in the last ten years.
answer_text: Over the 10 years from 1st April 2014 to 31st March 2024, Invest NI offered financial assistance of £12,581,644 to Moy Park Ltd, with £12,001,867 draw down by the company over this period. Financial support offered was in the form of:Selective Financial Assistance to support the creation of 628 new jobs and capital spend to generate production efficiencies;Grant for Research & Development activities;Growth Accelerator Programme support to develop new export markets;Skills support to train and upskill over 1,200 staff across the business.Over the same period, Invest NI provided other financial assistance of £8,280 comprising trade advisory services to support new market development.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the amount of rates relief Moy Park has received in the last ten years.
answer_text: The table below details rate relief on Non-Domestic properties where Moy Park Ltd is the named ratepayer, for the last ten rating years.Rating YearRate Relief2014/15£763,9632015/16£837,5972016/17£715,3792017/18£724,5372018/19£740,0642019/20£753,4322020/21£1,025,7062021/22£995,2512022/23£740,6362023/24£808,730Rate relief for the properties includes Industrial Derating, Small Business Rates Relief, Business Rates Holiday, NDVR Exemptions and District Rate Convergence. Properties with Industrial Derating applied received 12 months Business Rates Holiday in the 2020/21 and 2021/22 rating years and one month Business Rates Holiday in 2022/23.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister as part of their work on international relations, in relation to securing investment, trade and tourism, whether any concerns have been raised with them about whether any Belfast airport is used by military planes to fly weapons to Israel.
answer_text: The operation of military aircraft and its use of airports is not a devolved matter, and does not fall under the remit of the First Minister and deputy First Minister.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 1425/22-27, to detail the number of appointments specifically about lactation, or emphasising the benefits of breastfeeding, that were held in each of the last five years, broken down by Belfast constituency.
answer_text: This information is not held centrally, and the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust has advised that it does not maintain record logs of such interactions.The Royal Jubilee Maternity Service Belfast is a UNICEF "Baby Friendly" accredited service, a significant component of which is the support given to mothers to breastfeed new-born babies. Breastfeeding is discussed at multiple stages throughout a pregnancy journey by midwives with women in their care. The Belfast Health and Social Care Trust has Infant Feeding Midwife Specialists on staff who provide monthly virtual opt-in breastfeeding workshops. Specialist assistance and teaching is provided for mothers of twins, mothers whose babies may have special needs, or where mothers are diabetic. The Infant Feeding Team also provide a one-to-one lactation support/education session when needed, or if there have been previous feeding difficulties, or where the baby has needed care in the neonatal unit.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any discussions he has had with (i) RTE; (ii) TG4; and (iii) any other relevant bodies in relation to making Irish language television programmes, which are available in the south, available to viewers in the north.
answer_text: I am very supportive of maximising the coverage of Irish language programming in the North, however airing decisions are matters for individual broadcasters.My Department plays an important role in supporting the development of Irish language programmes via the Irish Language Broadcast Fund (ILBF) which is delivered by NI Screen. This Fund supports a broad genre of content from children’s shows to factual entertainment, educational and drama content.With an annual budget of just over £4 million, the ILBF supports local production companies to produce television, digital and audio content primarily for broadcast on TG4, BBC NI and RTÉ as well as on digital platforms.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of people that have been seriously injured, disabled or died due to not having access to a thrombectomy outside the hours of 8:00am - 6:00pm in the last five years.
answer_text: The expansion of the regional thrombectomy service to 24/7 remains a key priority in Northern Ireland.  However, the budget for Health which you supported, is insufficient to fund this service development.As the service is not commissioned, there is no data collected for the period 6:00pm to 8:00am. The current evidence base suggests that 10% of ischaemic stroke patients would be eligible for thrombectomy, of which 50% would be functionally independent following the procedure. Therefore, we can project that a further 82 patients may have benefited from thrombectomy in 2023 if a 24/7 service was in place, of which 41 would have regained functional independence. I continue to be committed to ensuring as many people as possible from across Northern Ireland continue to benefit from the service within the current hours of operation.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of women recorded as giving birth not in a hospital setting or at home in the last five years.
answer_text: We can provide the number of babies born before arrival to hospital services as detailed below however this may include some women who have had their baby at home before attending maternity services. Location of birth20182019202020212022Born before arrival111123142155110Not known<5<5Grand Total111142155*(data for 2023 is not complete due to Encompass rollout)

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health why thrombectomy services do not operate on a 24/7 basis.
answer_text: Given the budget, we are not currently in a position to operate on a 24/7 basis. This would require significant investment including the recruitment of specialist posts within a limited workforce pool. However, I am committed to ensuring as many people as possible from across Northern Ireland continue to benefit from the service within the current hours of operation.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the number of applicants to the Home to School transport service that were turned down in the last 12 months due to living below the distance threshold, but have (i) no access to a car; and (ii) limited mobility.
answer_text: My Department is responsible for the Home to School Transport Policy and the Education Authority (EA) for the operation and delivery of school transport, which includes determining eligibility to transport assistance.The Education Authority (EA) has advised that it does not hold the information on accessibility to cars.The Education Authority (EA) has also advised that it does not hold details on limited mobility.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any human rights considerations that were given to his Department’s consideration to introduce body cameras as part of its pilot scheme.
answer_text: The Belfast Trust is currently planning for the implementation of body worn cameras as a pilot initially.  As part of this planning phase, the Trust will engage with staff, trade unions and some lay representatives to draft a policy which will then be subject to a public consultation.  This will take place in 2025 and it is envisaged the pilot will commence in May 2025.  The Trust commits, as part of the public consultation, to do a full equality and human rights impact assessment.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust has carried out consultation or engagement with parents on the need for neonatal services to be available in Irish.
answer_text: The Neonatal Service in Belfast Health and Social Care Trust has an active Parent Engagement Forum.The Neonatal Service has not carried out a consultation on the need for the Neonatal Service to be delivered in Irish. This has never been raised as a request via the Parent Forum nor via any other avenue with the Neonatal Service.However, should language interpreters be required, the Neonatal Service has access to a supportive Interpreting Service.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department collects data on the number of children with Irish as their first language who received appointments through Irish from their health visitor.
answer_text: Neither my Department, nor the Health and Social Care Trusts, collect this data.  Health and Social Care Trusts may use translation services, where needed, to assist service users in receiving necessary care or support.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the schools in Lagan Valley that are deemed eligible for payments from the RAISE programme.
answer_text: No school has been deemed eligible for payments from the RAISE programme to date and no funding has been allocated.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the circumstances under which additional schools may be added to the list of schools eligible for RAISE funding.
answer_text: RAISE is about promoting a whole community approach to education through place-based partnerships.  The funded interventions will be expected to improve educational outcomes for children and young people living in areas of disadvantage through joint working across schools, communities and wider partnerships. No schools have been deemed eligible or ineligible for funding. Rather schools that serve the localities have been identified to inform a co-design process, however this does not preclude other stakeholders (including schools) from engaging in the process and informing strategic plans for the area.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) who can avail of bowel cancer screening; and (ii) when they can avail of it.
answer_text: In Northern Ireland, anyone aged 60-74 years old, who is registered with a GP, is eligible for bowel screening and is automatically sent a screening test kit in the post every two years.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for his assessment of the projected (i) cost; and (ii) time frame associated with bringing the average vacant domestic property up to a standard fit for habitation.
answer_text: My Department is not engaged in the redevelopment of vacant domestic properties therefore I cannot provide an assessment of the projected costs and timeframes associated with bringing the average vacant domestic property up to a standard fit for habitation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the organisations that were represented at the two-day Strategic Insights Lab held in June 2024, where social housing delivery and funding was discussed.
answer_text: In the table below I have provided details of the organisations that were represented at the two-day Strategic Insights Lab that was held in June 2024. OrganisationDepartment for CommunitiesNorthern Ireland Housing ExecutiveWoven Housing Association – Representing NIFHARadius Housing AssociationClanmil Housing AssociationApex Housing Association Choice Housing AssociationConstruction Employers Federation (CEFNI)Kelly Brothers as Chair of CEFNI Social Housing CommitteeDepartment for InfrastructureChartered Institute of HousingNI Local Government AssociationRural Community NetworkNI Water Derry City & Strabane District CouncilBelfast City CouncilLand & Property ServicesDepartment of FinanceStrategic Investment BoardUlster University

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education how schools that have been omitted from the list of those eligible for RAISE funding can still be involved in the programme.
answer_text: RAISE is about promoting a whole community approach to education through place-based partnerships.  The funded interventions will be expected to improve educational outcomes for children and young people living in areas of disadvantage through joint working across schools, communities and wider partnerships. No schools have been deemed eligible or ineligible for funding. Rather schools that serve the localities have been identified to inform a co-design process, however this does not preclude other stakeholders (including schools) from engaging in the process and informing strategic plans for the area.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on his phased approach to the introduction of social inclusion strategies, including (i) a timeline for the publication; and (ii) the strategies that are being prioritised for Executive approval.
answer_text: I will bring a draft Anti-poverty Strategy to the Executive in early 2025 for consideration. Following Executive agreement of this paper the Strategy will be subject to public consultation prior to its final publication. I wrote to my Executive colleagues in October 2025 to outline the next steps in developing the Disability Strategy, with a view to bringing a draft for Executive approval in early 2025. Proposals for the Gender Equality and Sexual Orientation Strategies will follow later in due course. 		While the Department for Communities is leading on the development of the Strategies, final decisions on its content, publication and implementation will be subject to Executive agreement and public consultation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he is considering exempting asylum seekers and survivors of human trafficking from proposed SmartPass application fees.
answer_text: Asylum seekers in receipt of asylum support will be exempt from the new SmartPass fees. Victims of human trafficking are not included in the exemption at this time.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, in light of the Planning Appeals Commission’s letter of 16 October 2024 to councils in relation to LA03/2021/0940/F, whether his Department will declare the application to be of regional significance.
answer_text: The application was not submitted under the provisions of section 26 of the Planning Act (NI) 2011 which would allow for a determination of regional significance but instead under the provisions of section 54 of the Act. This provision allows for development of land without compliance with conditions previously attached. My Department is unable to reconcile the statement from the Commission that the Department does not have the powers to refer the application to the Commission and in a written reply, has helpfully drawn the Commission’s attention to the relevant provisions.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any consideration they have given to including death, dying and bereavement as a strategic priority in the Programme for Government.
answer_text: The draft Programme for Government sets out the Executive’s proposed priorities and does not include everything that each department is working to deliver. Now that public consultation has closed, a consultation report will be provided before the consideration of the Executive. This will help us determine how best to reflect our collective priorities in the final draft of the Programme for Government.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the work he has carried out to implement a Blood Cancer Action Plan.
answer_text: Whilst my Department acknowledges the helpful 17 recommendations outlined within the UK Blood Cancer Action Plan, it should be stated that it was not consulted or involved in the development of this Action Plan. However, the recommendations around the themes of workforce, diagnosis, care, treatment, and access to clinical trials and data, are all areas we acknowledge require work, and we have been working to address these via the NI Cancer Strategy 2022 to 2032. Blood cancer /haemato-oncology services in NI have been under growing pressures for some time amidst serious workforce concerns for haematological services UK wide.  In order to address the severe regional pressures within haematology services, in 2023 my NI Cancer Policy and Projects Team initiated a project for the redesign of Haematology Treatment Pathways across NI to include the three main haematological pathways: Myeloma, Lymphoma and Leukaemia. Three reports with recommendations were completed in March 2024.The Strategic Planning and Performance Group (SPPG) of my Department were asked to implement the recommended actions of these reports in April 2024. SPPG have engaged with Trusts on the reports’ recommendations.  Some progress has been made on early stage actions including, baseline assessment and identification of nursing workforce gaps, maximising virtual clinics, identifying appropriate time and process for holistic needs assessment, weekly multi-disciplinary Team (MDT) meetings, adoption of NICE guidance and development of a haematology cancer nurse career pathway. Many actions across all 3 reports are dependent upon reform and greater skill mix within haematology services. While some recommendations are cost neutral, others require investment, and resource to implement.  Progression of some key actions is dependent on consultant recruitment and retention as well as additional investment in greater skill mix for the nursing workforce to include more Advanced Nurse Practitioners.  This will help create capacity to take forward a number of the recommended actions.   Action 16 of NI Cancer Strategy 2022 - 2032 is to develop a specialist integrated haematological diagnostics service (SIHMDS) for Northern Ireland. This will provide more timely, co-ordinated and equitable diagnosis for people presenting with blood cancers and will be taken forward when funding becomes available.Clinical trial uptake is low in Northern Ireland and this is acknowledged in the NI cancer strategy under Actions 55 and 56. Work will be undertaken to address these actions over the lifetime of the NI Cancer Strategy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether an assessment has been made of how dilapidated buildings in Belfast city centre could be used for social housing.
answer_text: The powers to address dilapidated buildings fall under the responsibility of the Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (DAERA).  DAERA officials have advised it is their intention to introduce a bill in November that seeks to deal with dilapidation at a much earlier stage and address a significant legislative deficit in Northern Ireland compared with other jurisdictions. Whilst no specific assessment has been made by my Department into the use of dilapidated buildings for social housing in Belfast city centre, my draft Housing Supply Strategy recognises the need for further action to support the rating and planning systems to ensure we can make better use of underused assets for domestic use.Furthermore, Belfast City Council has recently launched a factfinding programme called H.O.U.S.E. (Homes On Upper Spaces for Everyone). The Council is asking for information from property owners, owner occupiers, investors and developers who have unused spaces on upper floors within the city centre to determine the level of interest to bring forward current vacant upper floor spaces for residential use and understand the enablers required to do this. Department officials will be briefed on the outcome of this exercise and subject to its findings, will consider if there is opportunity to align this work with existing and developing Departmental housing and regeneration policies and programmes.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to outline how many groups he has declined to meet since taking office.

To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, in light of the Planning Appeals Commission's letter on 16 October 2024 in relation to the Dalradian conjoined public inquiry, whether his Department will seek the further environmental information from the planning applicant under the relevant provisions of the Planning (Environmental Impact Assessment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015 and 2017.
answer_text: Officials have advised me that a letter was submitted to the Planning Appeals Commission and the Department on 17 October 2024 from Cleaver Fulton Rankin on behalf of Dalradian Gold Limited, advising of the applicant’s intention to consider in its Statement of Case submission the matters highlighted in the correspondence of 16 October 2024 with regard further information.My Department will not be seeking Further Environmental Information under the relevant provisions of the Planning (Environmental Impact Assessment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015 and 2017 at this time.This approach is consistent with the 2015 and 2017 EIA Regulations.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the Education Authority's review of the temporary arrangements for children living in Housing Executive non-standard accommodation.
answer_text: The Education Authority’s (EA) Intercultural Education Service (IES) continue to play a leading role in the education sector’s response to children arriving through Asylum and Refugee processes and provide an invaluable service to such families.  The temporary arrangements for children seeking a school place who are housed in NIHE non-standard accommodation or initial hotel contingency accommodation remains in place until the end of October and will be reviewed in early November and any revised service delivery arrangements will be agreed between my Department and the EA at that point.Significant progress has been made in recent months to reduce the length of time spent in such accommodations and current trends now show families receive an offer of temporary community-based accommodation within a short period of time.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether any (i) justice; and (ii) human rights concerns have been raised with her Department in relation to United States of America military planes travelling through Belfast International Airport.
answer_text: No justice or human rights concerns have been raised with my Department in relation to United States of America military planes travelling through Belfast International Airport.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any assessment his Department has made in relation to the frequency of United States of America military flights passing through Belfast International Airport.
answer_text: My Department holds no information in relation to this matter.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any information his Department holds in relation to the passage of United States of America military planes through Belfast International Airport.
answer_text: My department does not hold any information relating to the passage of United States of America military planes through Belfast International Airport.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any (i) regulations; and (ii) legislation that is in place to prevent the Housing Executive from building homes.
answer_text: There are no regulations or legislation to prevent NIHE from adding to new social housing supply.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the funding allocated for the Preventative Keeping Safe education Programme.
answer_text: My department does not currently provide funding for the preventative Keeping Safe education programme. I understand that other preventative education programmes, such as NSPCC’s Speak out Stay safe, are available to schools directly.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 15761/22-27, to detail the number of (i) ad-hoc; and (ii) regular planned meetings occurred.
answer_text: There have been:21 ad hoc meetings between the Translink Belfast Transport Hub project team and the Department’s authorisation team to provide advice and guidance on completing the authorisation process, and 71 regular planned meetings, including: stage gate reviews and monthly updates between Translink and the Department’s Translink Support Unit, and monthly updates in 2024 between the Translink Belfast Transport Hub project team and the Department’s authorisation team.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any attempts being made to map data or gather information in relation to the cost of poor housing and housing-related issues across all Executive Departments.
answer_text: My Department continues to engage and work across Executive departments to share information to help inform wider housing policy and specific interventions.  Examples of this include the Housing Supply Strategy, Interdepartmental Homelessness Action Plan and Supporting People Programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the average percentage increase in pay for a (i) classroom assistant; (ii) general assistant 1; (iii) general assistant 2; and (iv) special educational needs classroom assistant, as part of the pay and grading review settlement.
answer_text: The increase that an individual will receive from the pay and grading review will depend on how their current pay point assimilates to the new pay scales.  Full details of this are contained on the DE website at:https://www.education-ni.gov.uk/articles/pay-related-issuesThe table below provides an indication of this increase by detailing the percentage increase at the top of the scale for each of the posts requested.Post% Increase between current top of scale and new top of scaleGeneral Assistant 18.6%General Assistant 218.3%Classroom Assistant6.9%Classroom Assistant SEN14.4%

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the number of fee-paying schools that avail of the RAISE programme.
answer_text: No school has availed of the Raise programme to date as no funding has been allocated. The areas and schools that are to be included within the scope of the Raise programme are subject to discussion at a locality level.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the Irish-medium schools that avail of the RAISE programme.
answer_text: No school has availed of the RAISE programme to date as no funding has been allocated.The areas and schools that are to be included within the scope of the Raise programme are subject to discussion at a locality level.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, in light of the increased building of student accommodation and office blocks, to detail the water infrastructure issues preventing the building of social homes.
answer_text: I am advised by NI Water that there are issues with lack of, or limited capacity across large parts of our wastewater infrastructure.  In some areas, NI Water are no longer able to permit any new foul discharge into the wastewater network.The issues with capacity vary according to where the development is located and the nature and scale of the proposed development.  NI Water considers developer’s proposals on a site-by-site basis and works with developers through its pre-planning process to facilitate connections where possible.  This may require some measure of developer led intervention.In all cases, NI Water requires developers to engage as early as possible with them regarding their proposal.  This will allow NI Water to support the development proposal where possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the rationale for no longer employing the method of using the percentage of free school meal entitled pupils in a school when determining eligibility for the RAISE programme.
answer_text: The methodology used to select RAISE areas reflects findings and recommendations from the work of the Expert Panel which published its report ‘A Fair Start’ in June 2021. It also reflects the discussions which took place with the Stakeholder Reference Group, established to support the “A Fair Start” Programme Board between October 2021 and September 2023.A range of data sets were analysed to bring forward a comprehensive set of child focused indicators which incorporate educational disadvantage factors and those relating to socio-economic disadvantage including poverty, health and crime/anti-social behaviour. Areas of disadvantage have been identified using these indicators. Free School Meal Entitlement has been included as one of the seven indicators. The programme will focus on where children live, not where they go to school.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail (i) whether Tarasis Housing is classed as a Housing Association; (ii) the number of homes they have; and (iii) any grants or funding his Department has provided Tarasis, or its predecessor Homecare, since 2018.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive, and I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive and been advised that:“Tarasis Housing (formally Homecare) is a private limited company operating under Tarasis Enterprises and is registered with Companies House.The Northern Ireland Housing Executive does not hold information on the number of homes they have.The table below includes the breakdown of identifiable expenditure to Tarasis by the Northern Ireland Housing Executive since 2018 by accounting year.2018/192019/202020/212021/222022/232023/242024/25*£k's£k's£k's£k's£k's£k's£k'sTotal Expenditure4,0202,3413,1095,1147,31810,1716,470Notes*2024/25 figures are to 16th October 2024.”These figures include costs associated with the provision of temporary accommodation such as a commercial rent charge which may include rent, rates and management fees as well as costs associated with the provision of support to vulnerable households in non-standard temporary accommodation settings and to those with tenancies at risk of breakdown.They also include refugee programme accommodation costs although these are separately funded through the Home Office on a scheme-by-scheme basis. Similarly, the figures for 2018/19 and 2019/20 also include costs associated with the Housing Executive’s contracted provision of Asylum Seeker Accommodation on behalf of the Home Office.” For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the NIHE directly.  Making direct contact with the ALB will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the amount of funding his Department has provided to Housing Associations since 2002.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive. I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“The Housing Executive has been responsible for the management of the Social Housing Development Programme (SHDP) since 2007. The information provided for this response therefore relates to the period 2007/08 – 2023/24 (and 2024/25 to-date). The total SHDP Budget spend during the period 2007/08 – 2023/24 (and 2024/25 to-date) is £2.283bn. Please note that the SHDP Budget spend is utilised to deliver new housing association Starts/Completions, but this figure also includes other funding to housing associations (e.g. Disability Adaptations Grant, Voluntary Purchase Grant). The total Grant payments made to housing associations during the period 2007/08 – 2023/24 (and 2024/25 to-date) is £2.279bn. Please note that the Grant payments to housing associations support new social housing Starts/Completions, but this figure also includes other funding to housing associations (e.g. Disability Adaptations Grant, Voluntary Purchase Grant).The Housing Executive has administered the Supporting People (SP) programme since 2003/04.  Records of payments from 2004/05 onwards are held. This response therefore relates to the period 2004/05 – 2023/24 (and 2024/25-date). Total SP Programme funding paid to Housing Associations by the NIHE during the period 2004/05 – 2023/24 (and 2024/25-date) is £265m”.In addition to the amounts above, my Department has also directly allocated £577,078,007 to Housing Associations between the period 2008/09 to 2024/25 (Year To Date). This accounts for £285,739,000 in Financial Transactions Capital (FTC) and £291,339,007 in grants and other funding. We are unable to provide records prior to 2008 as these have been destroyed, in accordance with the Department’s Disposal of Records Schedule, under the Public Records Act (NI) 1921.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the car parking available for Translink staff working at Belfast Grand Central Station.
