answer_text: There are currently a small number of designated car parking spaces for staff who work outside of the public transport network’s operational hours. On completion of the Belfast Grand Central Station development in 2025, there will be over 200 car parking spaces available for staff and public.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the schools in Lagan Valley that avail of the RAISE programme.
answer_text: No schools in Lagan Valley currently avail of the RAISE programme.  No funding has been allocated, or provisionally allocated, to any school or delivery partner.  Work on programme design is ongoing and will continue to evolve as engagement with stakeholders continues.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the number of social homes that have been built by Housing Associations between 2002 and 2024.
answer_text: In the table below I have provided details of the number of social housing starts for the period requested. At present the delivery of social housing is measured in terms of starts. For SHDP new build units are considered started when a housing association is able to demonstrate that a contract has been entered into and the contractor has taken possession of the site. question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the availability of in-house counselling services in GP surgeries in West Belfast in each of the last five years.
answer_text: The Northern Ireland Local Enhanced Service (NI LES) for Mild to Moderate Depression enables practices to employ practice-based counsellors to provide counselling for patients (aged 18 years and above) with mild to moderate depression. This service ensures that patients receive care from competent practitioners for mild to moderate depression with low intensity psychosocial interventions such as individual guided self-help based on the principles of cognitive behavioural therapy (CBT).The NI LES Mild to Moderate Depression service is an enhanced service which covers enhanced aspects of clinical care of a patient - all of which are beyond the scope of essential General Medical Services. They are therefore, not mandatory and as independent contractors GP Practices have the choice to contract to provide this service or not.There are 16 GP Practices in West Belfast. The following table outlines the number of GP Practices in West Belfast which have opted to contract the service over the last five years.YearGP Practices2020/202182021/202262022/202382023/202462024/20256

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of patients currently availing of Eye Movement Desensitisation and Reprocessing treatment in the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: BHSCT Mental Health ServicesEye Movement Desensitisation and Reprocessing (EMDR) treatment is not currently a standalone service within Belfast Trust.  Therefore, data pertaining to numbers of patients availing of this treatment modality is not available within the current reporting structures.BHSCT - Psychological ServicesPsychological services do not record information in this way. To provide the information would require a manual trawl of records.  The information could therefore only be supplied at disproportionate cost.EMDR is not a treatment for which there is a direct referral path. Service Users are referred due to presenting mental health difficulties and psychological distress and, as part of their assessment and intervention, the potential for EMDR work will be considered when appropriate, alongside other areas of intervention and support.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any engagement he or his officials have had with the Beyond Pills All Party Parliamentary Group.
answer_text: The Department of Health leads on Preventing Harm, Empowering Recovery – the Northern Ireland Executive’s cross-departmental strategic framework for tackling the harm from alcohol and other drugs, which includes reducing the harms associated with the misuse of prescription medicines.The misuse of prescription medication has been shown to be associated with a wide range of substance use related harms. As well as people illicitly seeking out prescription medicines, there can also be issues with involuntary addiction to prescription medications if they are not taken or prescribed in line with guidelines. There are also cases where individuals try to access medication online if prescriptions they feel they require are not being increased or are stopped too quickly.Preventing Harm Empowering Recovery includes an action for the Department’s Strategic Planning & Performance Group to produce an updated Prescription Drug Misuse Action Plan which, building on the current processes, will include additional support to monitor prescribing levels and support for prescribers to better understand who may be at risk of harms. This will include raising awareness of the harms associated with the illicit use of prescribed medicines and with polydrug use, including promoting awareness across primary and secondary care healthcare providers. This work will commence following the imminent publication of the Substance Use Strategic Commissioning and Implementation Plan.At present, my Department has not had any engagement with the Beyond Pills All Party Parliamentary Group.  I do however welcome the APPG’s objectives for reducing Prescribed Drug Dependence, as these would appear to closely mirror the work already underway in the Department on this issue. We will continue to look at what further action can be taken locally, and in collaboration with others across the UK and elsewhere, to address this important issue.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for his assessment of the Participation and the Practice of Rights' five steps approach to mental health.
answer_text: I am aware that the Participation and the Practice of Rights recently launched its five steps approach to mental health entitled ‘Give 5: Steps to a Wellbeing Rights based Framework’, which sets out what it believes government and organisations can do to better tackle the mental health crisis.  The themes of the framework are broadly aligned with the Mental Health Strategy 2021-31 which sets out the future strategic direction of mental health services for Northern Ireland.  The Strategy, which includes 35 actions set out under three overarching themes, is person-centred and takes a whole life and whole system approach.  The key aim of the Strategy is to ensure long-term improved outcomes for the mental health of the population, recognising that a person’s mental health is shaped by a range of social, economic, cultural and environmental factors and notes the need for a joined up approach across government, sectors and the whole of society to implement existing policies designed to address deprivation, poverty, loneliness and social cohesion issues, and other social determinants of mental ill health.Furthermore, it notes that the vision for Northern Ireland is a society which promotes emotional wellbeing and positive mental health for everyone with a lifespan approach, which supports recovery, and seeks to reduce stigma and mental health inequalities. Implementation of the Strategy, when funding permits, will create a system that ensures consistency and equity of access to services, so that the individual and their needs are placed right at the centre, respecting diversity, equality and human rights, and ensuring people have access to the most appropriate, high-quality help and treatment at the right time, and in the right place.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 15497/22-27, to detail the number of Unsatisfactory Intermittent Discharges across (i) Belfast; and (ii) West Belfast.
answer_text: This is an operational matter for NI Water. However, I have sought the requested information  and have been advised as follows: Belfast Council AreaBelfast West ConstituencyUnsatisfactory Intermittent Discharges (UIDs)15840For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact NI Water directly, through the Elected Representatives Hotline on 0345 300 6461 or through waterline@niwater.comMaking direct contact with NI Water will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 15497/22-27, whether there is further relining work of Unsatisfactory Intermittent Discharges planned in West Belfast.
answer_text: The management of public wastewater infrastructure is an operational matter for NI Water. However, I have sought the requested information from NI Water and have been advised as follows:NI Water is not planning any further relining work in West Belfast at present.  This type of work is carried out as required, with NI Water responding to issues when and where they occur.  NI Water continues to carry out routine checks on Combined Sewer Overflows in the area.For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact NI Water directly, through the Elected Representatives Hotline on 0345 300 6461 or through waterline@niwater.comMaking direct contact with NI Water will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 15497/22-27, whether the works carried out in Donegal Road will ensure there will be no further flooding in the area.
answer_text: The management of public wastewater infrastructure is an operational matter for NI Water. However, I have sought the requested information from NI Water and have been advised as follows:The main driver for the repair of the sewer in the Falls Road area was to extend the life of the sewer. Following the discovery of the void under the road, which was caused by a large open joint in the sewer, NI Water surveyed the downstream sewer and found a large crack in the crown of the pipe, that extended for around 150m down the Donegall Road. These issues necessitated remedial work to protect the sewer from collapsing.  NI Water cannot guarantee that out of sewer flooding will never occur, but it continues to work to avoid such scenarios. For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact NI Water directly, through the Elected Representatives Hotline on 0345 300 6461 or through waterline@niwater.comMaking direct contact with NI Water will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice, further to her Department’s role in tackling paramilitary activity, for her assessment of whether Ministers should consult with representative bodies of paramilitary organisations when developing public policy.
answer_text: It is vital that Ministers do everything possible to ensure that paramilitaries play no part in influencing public policy decisions. That clearly includes ensuring that we do not meet or consult with their so-called representative bodies. My role, both as Justice Minister, and as lead Minister for the Executive’s Programme on Paramilitarism and Organised Crime, is underpinned by the Ministerial Pledge to which all Ministers commit when we take office. That pledge makes clear our individual and collective responsibility to, for example, challenge all paramilitary activity and associated criminality and to accept no authority direction or control on our political activities.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether his Department has carried out or commissioned scientific research into the possible impacts on climate from greenhouse gases being emitted from (i) the Mobuoy illegal landfill site; and (ii) other illegal landfill sites.
answer_text: No scientific research of impacts on climate from greenhouse gases being emitted from either the Mobuoy site or any other illegal landfill site, have been commissioned.Estimates of total Greenhouse Gas emissions from the regulated waste sector are published on an annual basis in the Northern Ireland Greenhouse Gas Inventory. The UK approach to calculating emissions of methane from landfills uses a “Tier 2” methodology based on national data on waste quantities, composition, properties and disposal practices over several decades. Data on waste consignments landfilled in Northern Ireland for the period 2008 to 2022 were provided by the Northern Ireland Environment Agency. In 2022, emissions from landfill sites were estimated to be 580.1 kilotonnes of carbon dioxide equivalent, a reduction of 69% from the 1990 Base YearThe forthcoming draft Climate Action Plan will set out how the policies and proposals are projected to reduce emissions with the aim of meeting Northern Ireland's proposed first carbon budget.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 15497/22-27, whether all sewer systems in West Belfast have a 375mm diameter.
answer_text: The management of sewer systems is an operational matter for NI Water. question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, pursuant to AQW 16133/22-27, and in relation to the applications determined as not eligible, whether there is a record of the (i) political belief of the applicant; and (ii) organisation the applicant may have been a member.
answer_text: In line with the Data Protection Act, only relevant information is gathered and stored by the Victims’ Payments Board. The political beliefs/affiliations of an applicant are not necessary for the purposes of the Board assessing eligibility for victims’ payments.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether his Department has been made aware of a danger to wildlife populations as a result of the expansion of the Natural World Products site.
answer_text: The Department regulates the Natural World Products facility on the Glenside Road under a number of authorisations namely P0341/10A, P0603/22A, WML35/05 and WML 35/19.I am aware of a report of an impact on wildlife at the Natural World Products site at Glenside Road. I am advised by my officials that, in line with statutory processes, the identified protected species have been relocated under a licence issued by my department and this matter has now been closed.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, given the September 2024 decision by Lisburn and Castlereagh City Council to approve application LA05/2023/0914/F for a landfill waste storage facility on site, to detail any extra measures his Department has taken to ensure there will be no increase in air pollution as a result of additional waste being present on site.
answer_text: The planning application LA05/2023/0914/F appears to make no reference to a “landfill waste storage facility”. It may be useful to contact the relevant planning department for any questions on this planning approval.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail any extra measures put in place to ensure there would be no increase of air pollution due to the increased capacity at the Natural World Products site.
answer_text: The Department regulates the Natural World Products facility on the Glenside Road under an IPPC permit number P0341/10A and The Pollution Prevention and Control (Industrial Emissions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2013 (IPPC regs).	The site permit P0341/10A has conditions within it to mitigate against negative environmental impacts. These conditions are not dependent on the capacity of the site.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether there is a need for extra mitigations and measures to decrease the amount of air pollution emanating as a result of increased tonnage at the Natural World Products site.
answer_text: The Department regulates the Natural World Products facility on the Glenside Road under an IPPC permit number P0341/10A and The Pollution Prevention and Control (Industrial Emissions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2013 (IPPC regs).The permit P0341/10A has conditions within it designed to mitigate against negative environmental impacts. Should non compliances with these conditions occur, enforcement action can be considered in line with the DAERA enforcement policy.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, pursuant to AQW 16133-22/27, for a breakdown of the reasons why applications were deemed ineligible.
answer_text: The Victims’ Payments Board, which makes decisions on any entitlement to payment under the Scheme, has advised that its system does not currently allow for a breakdown of the reasons why the applications were deemed ineligible.  The Board, however, has advised that to be eligible for payment under the Scheme, applications must meet all of the following criteria:	the injury (either physical or psychological) was as a result of a relevant incident and has resulted in permanent disablement;	the assessed degree of disablement as a result of the relevant injury is not less than 14%;	the relevant incident in which the injury was sustained took place in the UK, Europe or anywhere in Europe at a time when the applicant was a British citizen, born in Northern Ireland and have at least one parent who is a British citizen, Irish citizen or otherwise entitled to reside in NI. 	the relevant incident in which the injury was sustained took place on or after 1 January 1966, but before 12 April 2010.For the purposes of this Scheme, an injury may only be considered to be caused by a relevant incident if it is suffered by that person when:present at a relevant incident;present in the immediate aftermath of a relevant incident in which a loved one died or suffered an injury; responding, in the course of employment, to a relevant incident, in which the person reasonably believed a loved one had died or suffered significant injury. This includes preventing, mitigating or otherwise addressing the incident. Employment also includes service of the Crown. Where an application has been determined ineligible by the Board, it will be because it did not meet one or more of the above criteria. Applications with multiple relevant incidents may result in ‘split pathway’ decisions i.e. contain eligible and ineligible relevant incidents within one application. A determination made by the Board may be appealed within 12 months of the date of the determination. This can include an appeal against a decision regarding eligibility, degree of disablement or regarding adjustments / deductions made to an award.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail any consultations carried out with environmental organisations to ensure the protection of wildlife and biodiversity on the Natural World Products site.
answer_text: My Department has not carried out any consultations with environmental organisations in relation to the Natural World Products Glenside site. Consideration of wildlife and biodiversity in the area would have formed part of any planning and permitting applications.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, given the persistent and widespread nature of odour complaints surrounding the Natural World Products site, whether an assessment has been made on a risk to public health.
answer_text: The Department regulates the Natural World Products facility on the Glenside Road under an IPPC permit number P0341/10A and The Pollution Prevention and Control (Industrial Emissions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2013.The NIEA has not carried out a public health assessment for the Natural World Products site. The Public Health Agency (PHA) is the competent authority for public health matters, however the Department will engage with the PHA, as required, on potential impacts to health from odorous sites.  The PHA’s advice to anyone with concerns about their health is to seek advice from their GP.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, pursuant to the comments by the deputy First Minister during Question Time on 7 October 2024 stating Israel's right to defend herself, whether this is agreed Executive Office policy.
answer_text: There is no agreed TEO position on these matters.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he will ensure that the active travel spend required by the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022 will not be spent on road resurfacing projects.
answer_text: I can confirm that active travel funding within my Department is not being used to fund road resurfacing projects. In some cases it is necessary to alter and reprofile the existing carriageway, including replacing kerb lines and drainage, to facilitate the provision of high-quality active travel infrastructure along existing roads. As such, resurfacing is a necessary consequence of the active travel improvements. It would not be practical to wait many years for the road surface to deteriorate to the point where it would be prioritised for resurfacing before progressing active travel improvements.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he will ensure that his Department's measured active travel spend in the 2024-25 financial year (i) is spent solely on active travel; and (ii) will comply with all necessary auditing processes.
answer_text: My Department has taken a number of important steps to increase capacity to support the delivery of active travel projects, including filling key posts within the Active Travel team. My officials work closely with colleagues across the Department and delivery partners to ensure that active travel funding is spent appropriately and continue to review arrangements to ensure robust financial governance.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the amount Cloghan Point received in rates relief or exemptions in the last ten years.
answer_text: Property ID 1077170, Cloughan Point Oil Terminal has received £4,574,801.73 rate relief or exemptions in the last 10 years. This has been provided by:District Rate Convergence  £12,046.40Business Rates Holiday  £82,894.33Industrial De-Rating £4,479,861.00

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the number of children that attended primary education through English medium education due to no space being available in Irish medium primary education in the last seven years.
answer_text: The admissions process is administered by the Education Authority (EA).The EA has confirmed that there are no statistics available where a child attended an English medium education due to no space being available in Irish medium.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure how he will ensure that his Department’s active travel spend is in keeping with the intent of the active travel clause in the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022.
answer_text: Since the introduction of the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022, my Department has been developing plans to support increased investment in the active travel necessary to meet its requirements. This work has included the development of an Active Travel Delivery Plan which will complement the Belfast Cycling Network Delivery Plan (2022) and the Strategic Plan for Greenways (2016) providing a firm basis for the future prioritisation of active travel infrastructure investment and delivery. It is my intention to launch the public consultation for the Active Travel Delivery Plan on 13 November 2024. My Department has also taken a number of important steps to increase capacity to support the delivery of active travel projects including filling key posts within the Active Travel team. My officials work closely with colleagues across the Department and delivery partners to ensure that active travel spend is in keeping with the intent of the Act and continue to review arrangements to ensure robust financial governance. I have also increased the budget for active travel in the current financial year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when AQW 14628/22-27 will be answered.
answer_text: The answer to AQW 14628/22-27 issued on 8th October.Further to that answer, I can now confirm that the last of the platforms at Belfast Grand Central Station were handed over to Translink on 28th September 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether there is sufficient capacity for children currently in naíonra to move into Irish medium primary education in September 2025.
answer_text: Where oversubscription arises, a school can submit a Temporary Variation (TV) application to my department to increase its intake to meet demand.  If there is no alternative provision within the same sector, additional places are likely to be granted.As my department considers Irish-medium education to be a standalone sector, this means that additional places are likely to be approved at Irish-medium primary schools unless the children concerned can access an alternative Irish-medium primary school in their area.For the 2024/25 school year, there were only three Irish Medium primary schools that requested TVs. Gaelscoil Na mBeann received 16 additional places, Gaelscoil Eanna received four additional places and Scoil na Fuiseoige received one additional place.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the number of classroom assistants currently employed in Irish medium education.
answer_text: A total of 396 classroom assistants are currently employed within the Irish medium sector.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the number of appeals received for the Troubles Permanent Disablement Payment Scheme that were successful.
answer_text: As of week commencing 30th September 2024, the Victims’ Payments Board has received 9,942 applications since the Scheme opened on 31 August 2021.  82 appeals have been determined as successful by the Board. 67 have been deemed unsuccessful. To date, £59 million has been paid to victims since the scheme opened for applications on 31 August 2021.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether there are any scheduled piling works on the train lines at Belfast Grand Central Station.
answer_text: Translink do not have any scheduled piling works on the train lines at Belfast Grand Central Station.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether his Department have tested levels of bioaerosols omitted from the Natural World Products site.
answer_text: The Department regulates the Natural World Products facility on the Glenside Road under an IPPC permit number P0341/10A and The Pollution Prevention and Control (Industrial Emissions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2013.The Department has not tested levels of bioaerosols omitted from the Natural World Products Site.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health how many people in the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust area are paying for the delivery of prescriptions from pharmacies.
answer_text: The Strategic Planning and Performance Group (SPPG) funds community pharmacy to provide a range of commissioned HSC services through an agreed 3-year Commissioning Plan (2022-25). The Commissioning Plan was developed in partnership with Community Pharmacy Northern Ireland (CPNI), the representative body for community pharmacy contractors in Northern Ireland.Prescription delivery is not part of the current Commissioning Plan and is not a service that is commissioned by SPPG from community pharmacies; therefore, the Department does not hold any information in relation to the number of patients paying for the delivery of their prescriptions from community pharmacies.Decisions as to whether to offer, withdraw or charge for such non-commissioned services are a commercial matter for individual pharmacies.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the number of applications received for the Troubles Permanent Disablement Payment Scheme that were unsuccessful.
answer_text: As of week commencing 30th September 2024, the Victims’ Payments Board has received 9,942 applications since the Scheme opened on 31 August 2021.  703 applications have been determined as not eligible by the Board.1,318 applications have been determined as eligible by the Victims’ Payments Board, resulting in payments to eligible applicants totalling £59 million to date.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when rail services will be restored between Lisburn and Lanyon Place.
answer_text: Translink has commenced rail services from Belfast Grand Central on 13 October 2024 including rail services calling at stations between Lisburn and Belfast Grand Central, and between Belfast Grand Central and Lanyon Place.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the measures Translink has taken to prevent accidents involving those working on train lines in and around Ballymoney train station.
answer_text: Translink has a certified Safety Management System and rigorous processes to establish a Safe System of Work for all those working on the Northern Ireland rail network.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the number of inspections his Department has carried out in 2024 to ascertain whether the Natural World Products site has been in breach of pollution regulations.
answer_text: The Department regulates the Natural World Products facility on the Glenside Road under an IPPC permit number P0341/10A and The Pollution Prevention and Control (Industrial Emissions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2013.The Department has carried out 11 site inspections from the start of 2024 to the date of this question.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether his Department carries out unannounced site inspections to monitor air quality in the area surrounding the Natural World Products site.
answer_text: The Department regulates the Natural World Products (NWP) facility under an IPPC permit number P0341/10A, and The Pollution Prevention and Control (Industrial Emissions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2013.The Department carries out unannounced odour assessments in the vicinity of the NWP facility.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether his Department has consulted with medical professionals to ensure any odours emanating from the Natural World Products site are not a danger to public health.
answer_text: The Department regulates the Natural World Products facility on the Glenside Road under an IPPC permit number P0341/10A and The Pollution Prevention and Control (Industrial Emissions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2013.The Department  consulted with the Public Health Agency when processing the initial permit application.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether platforms 5 to 8 at Belfast Grand Central Station have required repairs to the concrete apron.
answer_text: Some of the precast copers on the ramps at the ends of the platforms had to have minor adjustments carried out to their alignment following post-installation checks.  This work was carried out prior to acceptance by Translink as part of the normal quality assurance processes for construction work and was all at the constructor’s cost.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the number of inspections his Department has carried out since 2018 to ascertain whether the Natural World Products site has been in breach of pollution regulations.
answer_text: The Department regulates the Natural World Products facility under an IPPC permit number P0341/10A and The Pollution Prevention and Control (Industrial Emissions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2013.The Department has carried out 31 site inspections from the start of 2018 up to the date of this question.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether the concrete apron foundations at Belfast Grand Central Station are deep enough to accommodate a 201 Class Locomotive and the De Dietrich set.
answer_text: All foundations at Belfast Grand Central Station are deep enough to accommodate a 201 Class Locomotive and the De Dietrich set.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether the clearance on all platforms at Belfast Grand Central Station were designed to the UK gauge or the Irish gauge.
answer_text: Clearances to the platforms at Belfast Grand Central Station are in accordance with the Northern Ireland Railways (NIR) standards.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether platform 1 at Belfast Grand Central Station was closed off due to falling stone cladding.
answer_text: Platform 1 at Belfast Grand Central Station has not been closed off due to falling stone cladding.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, given the persistent nature of complaints in relation to its site in West Belfast, why his Department has declined to use enforcement actions against Natural World Products.
answer_text: The department regulates the Natural World Products facility on the Glenside Road under an IPPC permit number P0341/10A and The Pollution Prevention and Control (Industrial Emissions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2013.The Department assesses reports of odour in conjunction with regular site inspections and odour surveys. If any non-compliances are identified, the Department will take action deemed necessary in line with its enforcement policy.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the evidence base for the decision to extend the Protect Life 2 Suicide Prevention Strategy.
answer_text: The decision to extend the Strategy was taken by the Permanent Secretary and endorsed by the former Minister. This was on the basis of challenges in relation to the budget, the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic having prevented full Strategy delivery and uncertainty regarding establishment of the Executive at that time. The three-year extension will allow more time for fuller implementation and for existing actions to be delivered. A review of the evidence relating to suicide prevention is included within the recently published Protect Life 2 Review Report.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether the Natural World Products site (i) has been in breach of pollution regulations; and (ii) has failed to operate within the conditions of its permit.
answer_text: The Department regulates the NWP site in accordance with its permit number P0341/10A and The Pollution Prevention and Control (Industrial Emissions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2013.The Department holds records of six non-compliances logged against the site’s permit this year.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he has plans to introduce a glass bottle deposit scheme to increase glass recycling.
answer_text: I am bringing in the Deposit Return Scheme for drink containers alongside other UK nations and this is due to go live in October 2027.  Polyethylene terephthalate (PET) plastic bottles plus aluminium & steel cans from 150ml to 3litres will be included in the scheme. I will keep glass recycling under review and continue to explore other options to deliver improvement, with the intention of assessing the position again when the DRS, as currently proposed, is fully operational.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the measures taken by his Department to reduce air pollution from the Natural World Products site.
answer_text: The department regulates the Natural World Products facility on the Glenside Road under an IPPC permit number P0341/10A and The Pollution Prevention and Control (Industrial Emissions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2013.The site permit has conditions relating to fugitive emissions to air as well as bioaerosols. These conditions are monitored and regulated under the above permit.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department has carried out any risk assessments in relation to the provision of a bus service for rail passengers.
answer_text: Translink conduct risk assessments relevant to the operation of bus services for rail passengers.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether all private hire buses used for rail substitution services meet disability access requirements, comparable to those of Translink and Bus Eireann.
answer_text: As part of the proposed contract for rail substitution service, all buses were intended to be fully accessible. Unfortunately, none of the successful private operators for the service were able to fully meet the accessibility requirements.  To address this, Translink has put in place a fully accessible contingency bus service, which staff deploy when requested by customers.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the reasons for the delay in publishing the report into the Review of Protect Life 2 Action Plan.
answer_text: While the Review was completed in June we needed time to consider the detailed information that the review produced. This is important as we wanted to ensure that the report is fair, robust and accessible.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the contingency plans for when the Enterprise service runs hourly from 7 October 2024, in circumstances were Belfast Grand Central Station is not operating rail services by this date.
answer_text: Translink is now making preliminary plans to commence rail services from Belfast Grand Central Station on Sunday 13th October 2024 and the launch of an hourly Enterprise service between the island’s two capital cities is being planned for Tuesday 29th October 2024. The current Enterprise timetable will therefore continue until the hourly service is implemented.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when the Review of Protect Life 2 Action Plan will be finished and the report published.
answer_text: The Review of the Protect Life 2 Action Plan undertaken by independent consultants is complete and was published on 27 September.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) how often his Department carried out progress reviews during the construction of Belfast Grand Central Station; and (ii) when issues were identified in relation to the delay in the commencement of rail services at Belfast Grand Central Station.
answer_text: My Department has had regular planned and ad-hoc meetings with Translink throughout the Belfast Grand Central station project. The station opening is being implemented in phases with bus services operating from 8 September 2024 and rail services becoming operational on the 13 October 2024. There has been no delay to the safety authorisation process to facilitate commencement of rail services.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for their assessment of how their Department’s work has reduced racism within communities.

To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) for his assessment of the issue of Northern Ireland Transport Holding Company board meeting minutes not being published for over a year; and (ii) to detail the rationale for the decision to not publish these minutes.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Translink.  I have sought the requested information and have been advised as follows:‘Translink aim to publish approved Board minutes on a regular basis. Unfortunately, due to an administrative oversight the minutes referred to had not been published. This has now been rectified and these minutes are now available on the Translink website’

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any discussions his Department and Translink have had with Irish Rail in relation to the commencement of the Enterprise service in October 2024.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Translink who along with Irish Rail jointly operate the Enterprise service and are in regular contact to develop plans to introduce an enhanced hourly service between Dublin and Belfast later this autumn.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) which agency approved the design for Belfast Grand Central Station; and (ii) when the design was approved.
answer_text: The design of the station was part of the planning application for Belfast Grand Central Station which was approved by my Department in March 2019.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the safety issues preventing rail passenger services operating between Belfast Grand Central Station and Lisburn.
answer_text: The line between Belfast Grand Central Station and Lisburn has been closed to facilitate the construction of the new station together with the associated new trackwork and signalling schemes. The Department is responsible for ensuring NI Railways’ regulatory compliance with the rail safety and interoperability regulations and grants authority for new infrastructure to enter into service. Officials are working through the process with the safety of the travelling passenger’s paramount. Once the authorisation process is complete it will then be for Translink to determine when rail service operations commence.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether there are any changes to the speed limit of trains when approaching Dargan Bridge.
answer_text: There are no changes to speeds on approach to the Dargan Bridge.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the number of complaints his Department received in relation to air pollution or noxious odours in West Belfast in 2024.
answer_text: As regards reports of odour or air pollution, the department has received 105 reports during 2024, concerning two NIEA regulated facilities within the West Belfast area.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the time frame for Translink to commence rail services from Belfast Grand Central Station.
answer_text: The Department is responsible for ensuring NI Railways’ regulatory compliance with the rail safety and interoperability regulations and grants authority for new infrastructure to enter into service. Officials are working through the process with the safety of the travelling passengers’ paramount. Translink have now announced that rail services will commence from 13th October.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether any concerns have been raised around disability access to trains at Belfast Grand Central Station.
answer_text: Belfast Grand Central Station is a station for everyone and has been developed in partnership with organisations including the Inclusive Mobility and Transport Advisory Committee (IMTAC), Royal National Institute of Blind People Northern Ireland (RNIB) and GuideDogsNI. Whilst rail services have not yet commenced from Belfast Grand Central Station, all the platforms and trains are fully compliant in terms of accessibility and operate using the same accessibility standards as other stations across the public transport network.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, pursuant to AQW 14325/22-27, whether the Cross-Departmental Working Group has raised any issues with the Home Office or relevant British Government ministers.
answer_text: Our officials work across operational and policy spheres to facilitate integration of and co-ordinate effective support for refugees and asylum seekers who arrive here, including regular engagement with Home Office.Any issues raised in the CDWG that sit within the remit of the Home Office will be conveyed using this existing mechanism for contact and communication.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to provide details of the discussions that took place at the meeting between Irish Rail and Translink on 23 September 2024.
answer_text: I am not aware of any meeting that took place between Irish Rail and Translink on 23 September 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the number of complaints his Department received in relation to odours from the Natural World Products site in 2024.
answer_text: The total number of reports of odour that the department has recorded up to the date of your request for this year is 95.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the number of complaints his Department has received in relation to odours from the Natural World Products site since 2018.
answer_text: The department regulates the Natural World Products facility on the Glenside Road under IPPC permit number P0341/10A. The total number of reports of odour that the department has recorded by year are as follows:YearNumber of reports2024 to date95202349202214020214The number of reports prior to 2021 are not readily available.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 14466/22-27, when the NISRA review will be published.
answer_text: The NISRA review of suicide statistics in Northern Ireland has been published and is available at: https://www.nisra.gov.uk/publications/review-suicide-statistics-northern-ireland#:~:text=The%20Northern%20Ireland%20Statistics%20and%20Research%20Agency%20(NISRA)%20and%20Coroner%E2%80%99s

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the number of complaints his Department has received in relation air pollution or noxious odours in West Belfast since 2018.
answer_text: The total number of reports of odour that the department has recorded by year are as follows:YearNumber of reports2024 up to 25/09/2024952023582022150202124The number of reports prior to 2021 are not readily available.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of medical negligence cases that currently have a Public Interest Immunity certificate attached.
answer_text: Neither the Department of Health nor its HSCNI bodies have any medical negligence cases which currently have Public Interest Immunity certificates attached.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the average number of additional hours Translink staff worked without overtime pay in each of the last 12 months.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Translink.  I have sought the requested information and have been advised as follows:“In relation to AQW 15499, All overtime hours require the agreement of management and staff.”“In relation to AQW 15501, Translink is not aware of staff members not receiving payment for overtime worked where this is part of their terms and conditions of employment. Translink contains a number of staff groups that operate under different contractual agreements e.g. bus and train drivers, maintenance staff and management, professional and technical.  To respond fully to this question we need to understand which group this question relates to.” For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact Translink directly at aqw.request@translink.co.uk.  Making direct contact with Translink will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the circumstances under which Translink staff can refuse to work overtime hours.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Translink.  I have sought the requested information and have been advised as follows:“In relation to AQW 15499, All overtime hours require the agreement of management and staff.”“In relation to AQW 15501, Translink is not aware of staff members not receiving payment for overtime worked where this is part of their terms and conditions of employment. Translink contains a number of staff groups that operate under different contractual agreements e.g. bus and train drivers, maintenance staff and management, professional and technical.  To respond fully to this question we need to understand which group this question relates to.” For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact Translink directly at aqw.request@translink.co.uk.  Making direct contact with Translink will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any action his Department will take in relation to outdated sewerage systems and the effect this has on the construction of social homes.
answer_text: In 2024-25 I have provided NI Water with a total budget allocation of almost £0.5 billion, representing just under 40% of the total non-ring-fenced budget for my Department. A fully funded PC21 investment plan, approved by the independent Utility Regulator limits new housing connections to 4500 units.  However, NI Water has advised that some 18,000 houses can be funded through developer contributions.My officials are looking at developer contributions to see if a change to legislation is required to allow developers to be able to contribute to wastewater treatment works and our sewerage infrastructure.  This policy is in the early stages and in the meantime, developers should continue to engage with NI Water to see if localised solutions can be found.    I am also introducing new legislation in relation to sustainable drainage systems to help deal with excess stormwater and reduce the pressure on the drainage infrastructure.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department has raised any concerns about the pipe infrastructure for sewage in the Falls Road area.
answer_text: The management of public wastewater infrastructure is an operational matter for NI Water. However, I have sought the requested information from NI Water and have been advised as follows:There are significant capacity issues within the wastewater infrastructure in the Falls Road area of Belfast due to the presence of a number of storm overflows (SOs) that discharge at a frequency that is greater than they have been designed for.  These are classified as Unsatisfactory Intermittent Discharges (UIDs).In March 2024, NI Water undertook a repair on the 375mm diameter sewer and relined approximately 160 metres of the sewer from the junction with the Falls Road. This work followed the discovery of a large void underneath the carriageway at the top of the Donegal Road. This was a significant investment of approximately £80k, extending the life of the existing sewer and ensuring that it continues to operate at optimum capacity.For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact NI Water directly, through the Elected Representatives Hotline on 0345 300 6461 or through waterline@niwater.comMaking direct contact with NI Water will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department is required to provide Translink with safety clearance before the rail element of the new Belfast Grand Central Station can become operational; and (ii) to detail any delay to this clearance.
answer_text: Under the Rail Interoperability Regulations 2011, the Department	is required to issue an authorisation for entry into service before Belfast Grand Central Station can open for rail passenger services. There has been no delay in this process; the regulations allow the Department up to four months to conduct a review of the application and issue authorisation however, the Department is currently targeting a four-week review period.  Once this is complete, it will be for to Translink to determine when they wish to commence rail services from Belfast Grand Central Station.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) for an update on the implementation of recommendation six of the Public Accounts Committee report on Planning in Northern Ireland; and (ii) to detail the time frame to introduce legislation to afford citizens their equal right of planning appeal for Environmental Impact Assessment developments.
answer_text: I would refer the member to the response provided to AQW/12058/22-27 and can confirm that my position remains as set out in that response.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, given his commitment to an all-island rail network, including the reopening of the rail link between Derry and Portadown, how his Department has resolved the issue of the extant planning permission granted for apartments on the disused rail line at Victoria Road, Derry.
answer_text: Planning application A/2014/0592/F has been through the full rigours of the local decision-making process, including a decision to refuse by Derry City and Strabane District Council and a subsequent appeal before the Planning Appeals Commission (PAC), an independent appellant body whose decision is final. The Department, as regional planning authority, had no role in this process. In respect of the impact of planning decision A/2014/0592/F on any future restoration of rail services between Derry and Portadown, it may present an obstruction if a rail line was to follow that route. However, I can confirm one of the key initial steps of the All-Island Strategic Rail Review will be to prioritise the recommendations and develop an implementation plan and my officials are currently considering how best to take this forward for the interventions identified for the North. Each intervention will be subject to further feasibility studies, appraisal, business case approval and environmental assessment before future investment decisions are taken.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, following the European Commission’s closure of EU Pilot (2015) 7640, why his Department has not implemented the ninth recommendation of the Mills Review.
answer_text: Recommendation 9 of the 2013 Mills Review recommended a change in planning policy in order to not allow the granting of retrospective planning permission for sand and gravel workings.  The statutory provisions of the planning system in the North, in common with other planning regimes, does provide for the grant of planning permission retrospectively.  In the case of retrospective planning applications for unauthorised development which should have been subject to environmental impact assessment (EIA), my Department published Development Management Practice Note 9A: ‘Unauthorised Environmental Impact Assessment Development’, which provides practical information and advice on the legal principles which apply to the management of unauthorised EIA development in the planning process, including the determination of retrospective applications in such circumstances.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, in light of his Department’s oversight of the design and introduction of the new planning portal, whether it has been designed with a private or hidden section where certain documents can be withheld from public access.
answer_text: The planning portal contains a function where documents can be withheld from being published on the planning register in certain circumstances. This could include, for example, matters of an environmentally or commercially sensitive nature, data protection or national security.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the work his Department and Translink are undertaking with the Office of Rail and Road in relation to Belfast Grand Central Station.
answer_text: The Department is responsible for ensuring NI Railways’ regulatory compliance with the rail safety and interoperability regulations and grants authority for new infrastructure to enter into service. The Office of Road and Rail is assisting the Department to meet its statutory obligations with respect to rail safety and interoperability by providing technical advice and support in assessing Translink’s application for authority to enter Belfast Grand Central Station into service.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for her assessment of the PSNI response to pro-Palestine demonstrations in Strabane and Belfast.
answer_text: ORAL QUESTION No 15 - AQO 884/22-27			ANSWERThe policing of demonstrations is an operational matter for the PSNI who is accountable to the Northern Ireland Policing Board. As I am committed to respecting the operational independence of the PSNI and the Policing Board, it would not be appropriate for me to comment.  You may, therefore, wish to raise this matter directly with the Chief Constable.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to outline the rationale for changing the 10J and 10K bus services in Belfast.

To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail (i) any consideration his Department has given as to how the remediation of Mobuoy illegal dump will be funded; and (ii) any discussions he has had with the Minister of Finance in relation to requesting additional funding from the UK Government for this remediation.
answer_text: There is no agreed preferred remediation option for the Mobuoy site and there will not be until a public consultation has completed. I will carefully consider my department’s statutory duties in protecting water quality, and as things progress, the wider role of the Executive and Assembly in the scrutiny and affordability of appropriate next steps.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether it was originally planned for Belfast Grand Central Station to fully open on 25 August 2024.
answer_text: It was planned to fully open Belfast Grand Central Station in early Autumn 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the number of Northern Ireland Civil Service (NICS) staff that did not receive back pay in the last year due to previously being agency staff in the NICS.
answer_text: Data is not collected on previous employment as part of pay award processing, so it is not possible to state how many Civil Service staff were formerly agency workers.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the work his Department is undertaking to enhance the rights of substitute teachers, including access to holidays and sick pay.
answer_text: A teacher engaged and paid for each day worked at a rate of 1/195 of the annual salary of a full-time permanent teacher receives a higher daily rate to account for holiday pay. Since 2004 the following statement has been included on the pay slips of supply teachers: ‘Holiday pay is included within your daily rate of pay calculated as annual salary over 195 working days’. Teachers’ (including supply teachers’) terms & conditions are negotiated and agreed by the Teachers’ Negotiating Committee (TNC) which comprises representatives of teachers’ employers and sectoral bodies, the Department of Education and the 5 main teachers’ unions. Decisions are communicated to teachers and their employers via TNC Circulars.Through the TNC a review of the use of supply teachers has been carried out and the recommendations from this review are currently being considered.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the work being carried out between Translink and the Rail Safety Branch in relation to Belfast Grand Central Station.
answer_text: As would be normal procedure, Rail Safety Branch officials, have been working closely with the Translink project team to ensure the application for authorisation of Belfast Grand Central Station meets the rail safety regulatory requirements under the Rail Interoperability Regulations 2011 and the Directive (EU) 2016/797 on the interoperability of the rail system, and to achieve authorisation for passenger services into Belfast Grand Central Station.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether there is any nuclear waste stored in rural areas.
answer_text: There are no nuclear facilities in Northern Ireland and there is no nuclear waste stored in Northern Ireland.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether the railway line between Lanyon Place and Lisburn will be reinstated whilst works are completed at Belfast Grand Central Station.
answer_text: The railway line between Lanyon Place and Lisburn has undergone upgrades and this new track layout would require authorisation into service. Translink’s application for authority to enter Belfast Grand Central Station into service also includes the line between Lanyon Place and Lisburn, which is currently in progress.  It would be a matter for Translink to consider if they want to apply for two separate authorisations to allow the line between Lanyon Place and Lisburn to open on a different timeframe to the opening of rail services to Belfast Grand Central Station.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any work they have carried out in order to support votes for migrants ineligible to vote here, including engaging with the Migrant Democracy Project.
answer_text: The Executive Office recognises the massive contribution our migrant and minority ethnic communities make to our society - not just to our economic, social and cultural life, but also to our political life too. Democratic rights are a key step to integration and encouraging participation from all communities is crucial. Our Racial Equality Subgroup recently met with representatives of The Electoral Commission and discussed challenges faced by minority ethnic communities with regards to voting, standing as candidates, and improving voter awareness, and will continue to engage with them.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the work his Department has carried out in order to support young people leaving care to obtain a home.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive. I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive and been advised that:“The Housing Executive continues to support young people leaving care via delivery of shared responsibilities with Health and Social Care Trusts towards meeting the accommodation needs of 16 and 17-year-olds which is set out in legislation and outlined within the NIHE/ HSC Regional Good Practice Guidance on Meeting the Accommodation and Support Needs of Young People aged 16-21. The Housing Executive has worked with a range of statutory and voluntary partners in the development of a Youth Homelessness Action Plan which has identified young people who have been part of the care system prior to reaching 16 years of age, and then become homeless at a later point, as one of three groups of young people who are central to the Action Plan. The Action Plan is due to be formally launched on 25th November 2025.The Housing Executive has also committed to the introduction of dedicated Housing Advisors and Team Leaders in all areas to take a lead in relation to young people presenting as homeless. Training has been facilitated with the Children’s Law Centre with these staff working in partnership with the relevant Health and Social Care Trust, to shape the service around the needs of the young person.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the NIHE directly.  Making direct contact with the ALB will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the incident level attributed to the removal of the plaque marking the opening of the Belfast City Hospital tower by Prince Andrew.
answer_text: The Belfast Health and Social Care Trust is responsible for this matter and reports to the Department. The Trust has advised that a Significant Event Audit (SEA) was carried out under the Trust’s Procedure for Serious Adverse Incidents.  An SEA is the lowest level of SAI.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the learning that was identified by (i) the health service; and (ii) the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust in relation to the removal of the plaque marking the opening of the Belfast City Hospital tower by Prince Andrew.
answer_text: The Belfast Health and Social Care Trust is responsible for this matter and reports to the Department. The Trust has advised as follows:The health serviceNo regional learning was identified.The Belfast Health and Social Care TrustThe Trust issued the following instruction:‘Please note that any request for alteration, removal, relocation, disposal of commemorative items in or on Trust property must not be actioned without prior review by Estates Senior Management Team and subsequent approval by the Trust’s Executive Team.Similarly, management permission must be sought for actions in respect of items of art and/or of heritage.’

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether the Workforce Strategy Action Plan will protect training and development of clinical oncology and radiology trainees.
answer_text: The commissioning of high-quality training and development is recognised as Theme 2 within the Second Action Plan (2022-23 to 2024-25) of the Health and Social Care Workforce Strategy 2026: Delivering for Our People.  Sustained investment in education and training is essential for ensuring that we have in place the optimum workforce, with the right skills and in the right numbers to deliver care to our population and undertake much needed service transformation.My Department reviews its commissioning of education and training annually, taking into account a range of factors including staffing and vacancy levels, recommendations from strategic workforce reviews and assessments of priorities and affordability.  However, no programmes are ring fenced.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the work his Department has carried out to expand clinical radiology speciality training places.
answer_text: In recent years, my Department has been able to increase the places it commissions on the clinical radiology training programme provided by the Northern Ireland Medical and Dental Training Agency by 46%; from 37 (2015/16) to 54 (2022/23).  My Department also intends to commission a further three training places on this programme once the necessary funding can be identified. In addition, my Department and the HSC are taking forward a proposal to develop a multi-professional Imaging Academy for Northern Ireland, to significantly increase further the supply of locally trained consultant radiologists over the longer term.  Progress on this initiative remains subject to business case approval and availability of funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether an adverse incident form was used as the investigation method in relation to the removal of the plaque marking the opening of the Belfast City Hospital tower by Prince Andrew.

To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for his assessment of the (i) criticisms; and (ii) recommendations in the Northern Ireland Audit Office's report, Public Bodies’ Response to Misrepresented Soil Analysis, in relation to potential agricultural planning fraud.
answer_text: I would refer the member to AQW 14981. In recent months my officials have met with senior management in the Northern Ireland Audit Office as well as representatives from the Society of Local Authority Chief Executives (SOLACE).  The aim of this work is to learn from the recent findings with the intention of moving forward collectively with local planning authorities on this issue.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any action his Department has taken in response to the Northern Ireland Audit Office's report, Public Bodies’ Response to Misrepresented Soil Analysis, since it's publication in July 2024.
answer_text: In recent months my officials have met with senior management in the Northern Ireland Audit Office as well as representatives from the Society of Local Authority Chief Executives (SOLACE).  The aim of this work is to learn from the recent findings with the intention of moving forward collectively with local planning authorities on this issue.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether weight restriction only applies to Enterprise trains at Belfast Grand Central Station.
answer_text: We do not anticipate any weight restrictions will apply to the Enterprise or any rolling stock at Belfast Grand Central Station.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) when Translink and his Department became aware of weight issues with the new rail tracks at Belfast Grand Central Station; and (ii) how long it will take to rectify the problem.
answer_text: There will be no weight issues impacting the new rail tracks at Belfast Grand Central Station.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to provide details on the weight issues impacting the new rail tracks at Belfast Grand Central Station.
answer_text: There will be no weight issues impacting the new rail tracks at Belfast Grand Central Station.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of health service workers from the Black, Asian and Minority Ethnic community that are on the board of a Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: The Trusts collect equality data, including ethnicity, for all members of HSC staff.  Staff are encouraged to provide this information but are not legally obliged to do so and as such the Trusts do not hold fully accurate data.  The Trusts collate and use overarching statistical data to promote equality best practice and to help inform decision making in line with its S75 responsibilities.  Where there are small numbers of staff in any identified pool the organisation cannot release the data in order to protect confidentiality and uphold data protection.All Trusts remain committed to promoting equality of opportunity and good relations.  They are also committed to celebrating diversity and treating everyone with dignity and respect.  Their aim, in line with the HSC Values, is to provide and promote inclusive, accessible and responsive services while celebrating diversity and the great benefits of shared experiences both of our staff and service users.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) the paediatric gastroenterologist teams that exist in each Health and Social Care Trusts; and (ii) any changes that have been made to these services in the last four years.

To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any conversations the Minister has had with any IT provider or software technology firms in relation to implementing a localised scheme to administer Winter Fuel Payments.
answer_text: I have not had any conversations with any IT provider or software technology firms in relation to implementing a localised scheme to administer Winter Fuel Payments.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether the GOV.UK portal could administer Winter Fuel Payments rather than acquiring a new IT system.
answer_text: GOV.UK is a website. It doesn’t have the functionality to process and issue welfare payments.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether any Health and Social Care Trust have had a Chief Executive from a Black, Asian and Ethnic Minority background.
answer_text: The Trusts collect equality data, including ethnicity, for all members of HSC staff.  Staff are encouraged to provide this information but are not legally obliged to do so and as such the Trusts do not hold fully accurate data.  The Trusts collate and use overarching statistical data to promote equality best practice and to help inform decision making in line with its S75 responsibilities.  Where there are small numbers of staff in any identified pool the organisation cannot release the data in order to protect confidentiality and uphold data protection.All Trusts remain committed to promoting equality of opportunity and good relations.  They are also committed to celebrating diversity and treating everyone with dignity and respect.  Their aim, in line with the HSC Values, is to provide and promote inclusive, accessible and responsive services while celebrating diversity and the great benefits of shared experiences both of our staff and service users.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the work his Department has undertaken to support young people leaving care to obtain a home.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive. I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive and been advised that:“The Supporting People programme, which is administered by the Housing Executive on behalf of the Department for Communities, provides grant funding to deliver housing related support services for young people, including young people who are care experienced, to live independently in the community. Housing related support is tailored to meet the needs of young people through accommodation-based provision for those in housing need. Floating support services help young people with a new tenancy to set up a new home and prevent homelessness. Support can include guidance and advocacy on housing options, assistance with finance and budgeting, developing practical skills to maintain a home and assistance with education, employment and mental wellbeing. The Housing Executive is due to publish a Youth Homelessness Action Plan in October 2024 which outlines a commitment to review existing guidance to ensure the needs of young people leaving care are met.” For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the NIHE directly.  Making direct contact with the ALB will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) to provide further detail on the plans for an Enhanced Investment Zone; and (ii) to detail how the allocated £150 million will be used.
answer_text: Investment Zones are a British government policy and the new Labour government has yet to confirm its position on the Enhanced Investment Zone (EIZ) for the North. A decision on this is expected in the October Budget announcement, at the earliest, however this may realise into the Spring announcement. I am unable to advise how the £150m will be used as we are at the early stages of the programme and these decisions have not been taken. My officials remain in contact with officials from the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government on the EIZ programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the action he is taking to reschedule the guarantor scheme.
answer_text: I have no plans to develop a guarantor scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the support, financial or otherwise, his Department is offering libraries in West Belfast.
answer_text: My department continues to support the network of six public libraries in the West Belfast constituency, providing Libraries NI with the resources to meet the day-to-day operational needs of the service and through investment in infrastructure and facilities. Over the past five years capital investment totalling some £424,000 has been provided to support specific facility improvement projects delivered by Libraries NI, as detailed in the table below:   LNI Capital Projects in West Belfast Constituency 2019 to 2024 Project 2019/20 2020/21 2021/22 2022/23 2023/24 Falls Road Library- damp proof works £37,000 Falls Road Library- upgrade to LED lighting £60,000 Falls Road Library-stonework restoration £117,000 Shankill Road Library-fire escape upgrade £54,000 Suffolk Library- upgrade to heating £24,000 Suffolk Library- Window upgrade £75,000 Whiterock Library – upgrade to heating plant £57,000 Total £303,000 £24,000 £97,000

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any consideration his Department has given to allowing the Housing Executive to build homes.
answer_text: NIHE has recently completed construction of an energy efficiency pilot project of six semi-detached, two-bedroom lifetime home dwellings at Sunningdale Gardens, Belfast, constructed to a standard beyond that of the current Building Regulations of Northern Ireland. The aim of this project is to explore the viability of combining thermal improvement measures with “Modern Methods of Construction”. This pilot is the first time that the Housing Executive has been directly involved in the construction of new homes in over 24 years.My Department and NIHE will work to analyse the project outcomes and financial details to develop a clearer understanding of improving energy efficiency measures in the sector and to assess the value for money considerations which will help inform where financial resources are best allocated in future social house building projects to ensure that public funds are utilised in the most effective way.I am actively pushing for a solution on NIHE Revitalisation that does not require reclassification or significant reform, but where the NIHE will have the ability to borrow so that it can invest in and maintain its current housing stock and to add to housing supply by building social homes.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the number of migrant, refugee and asylum-seeking families and advocacy groups that were consulted on the new Intercultural Education Service Arrangements for children living in Housing Executive Non-Standard Accommodation before it came into effect.
